1296: 1297: /** 1298: * Open a terminal window on the current platform. 1299: * Automatically detects the best terminal type and method. 1300: * 1301: * @param cwd - Working directory for the terminal 1302: * @param command - Optional command to execute 1303: * @param windowName - Optional window name (used for tmux) 1304: * @returns Success status and optional error message 1305: */ 1306: export async function openTerminal( 1307: cwd: string, 1308: argv?: string[], 1309: windowName?: string, 1310: options?: { 1311: detectTerminalType?: () => TerminalType 1312: openCmuxTerminalWithState?: ( 1313: cwd: string, 1314: argv?: string[], 1315: ) => Promise<CmuxTerminalExecutionResult> 1316: openPlatformTerminal?: (cwd: string, argv?: string[]) => Promise<TerminalResult> 1317: }, 1318: ): Promise<TerminalResult> { 1319: const terminalType = options?.detectTerminalType?.() ?? detectTerminalType() 1320: if (terminalType === "cmux") { 1321: const cmuxResult = await (options?.openCmuxTerminalWithState ?? openCmuxTerminalWithState)( 1322: cwd, 1323: argv, 1324: ) 1325: if (cmuxResult.terminalResult.success) { 1326: return cmuxResult.terminalResult 1327: } 1328: 1329: if (!cmuxResult.hasStateMutation) { 1330: return (options?.openPlatformTerminal ?? openPlatformTerminal)(cwd, argv) 1331: } 1332: 1333: return cmuxResult.terminalResult 1334: } 1335: 1336: return openTerminalByType(terminalType, cwd, argv, windowName) 1337: } 1338: 1339: async function openPlatformTerminal(cwd: string, argv?: string[]): Promise<TerminalResult> { 1340: const platformTerminalType = detectPlatformTerminalType() 1341: return openTerminalByType(platformTerminalType, cwd, argv) 1342: } 1343: 1344: async function openTerminalByType( 1345: terminalType: Exclude<TerminalType, "cmux">, 1346: cwd: string, 1347: argv?: string[], 1348: windowName?: string, 1349: ): Promise<TerminalResult> { 1350: if (terminalType === "tmux") { 1351: return openTmuxWindow({ 1352: windowName: windowName || "worktree", 1353: cwd, 1354: argv, 1355: }) 1356: } 1357: 1358: switch (terminalType) { 1359: case "macos": 1360: return openMacOSTerminal(cwd, argv) 1361: 1362: case "windows": 1363: // Check if we're in WSL 1364: if (process.platform === "linux" && isInsideWSL()) { 1365: return openWSLTerminal(cwd, argv) 1366: } 1367: return openWindowsTerminal(cwd, argv) 1368: 1369: case "linux-desktop": 1370: return openLinuxTerminal(cwd, argv) 1371: 1372: default: 1373: return { success: false, error: `Unsupported terminal type: ${terminalType}` } 1374: } 1375: } 1: /** 2: * background-agents 3: * Unified delegation system for OpenCode 4: * 5: * Replaces native `task` tool with persistent, async-first agent delegation. 6: * All agent outputs are persisted to storage, orchestrator receives only key references. 7: * 8: * Based on oh-my-opencode by @code-yeongyu (MIT License) 9: * https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode 10: */ 11: 12: import * as fs from "node:fs/promises" 13: import * as os from "node:os" 14: import * as path from "node:path" 15: import { type Plugin, type ToolContext, tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin" 16: import type { Event, Message, Part, TextPart } from "@opencode-ai/sdk" 17: import { adjectives, animals, colors, uniqueNamesGenerator } from "unique-names-generator" 18: import { getProjectId } from "./kdco-primitives/get-project-id" 19: import type { OpencodeClient } from "./kdco-primitives/types" 20: 21: // ========================================== 22: // READABLE ID GENERATION 23: // ========================================== 24: 25: function generateReadableId(): string { 26: return uniqueNamesGenerator({ 27: dictionaries: [adjectives, colors, animals], 28: separator: "-", 29: length: 3, 30: style: "lowerCase", 31: }) 32: } 33: 34: // ========================================== 35: // METADATA GENERATION (using small_model) 36: // ========================================== 37: 38: interface GeneratedMetadata { 39: title: string 40: description: string 41: } 42: 43: /** 44: * Generate title and description from result content using small_model 45: * Falls back to truncation if small_model unavailable 46: */ 47: async function generateMetadata( 48: client: OpencodeClient, 49: resultContent: string, 50: parentID: string, 51: debugLog: (msg: string) => Promise<void>, 52: ): Promise<GeneratedMetadata> { 53: const fallbackMetadata = (): GeneratedMetadata => { 54: // Fallback: truncate first line/paragraph 55: const firstLine = 56: resultContent.split("\n").find((l) => l.trim().length > 0) || "Delegation result" 57: const title = firstLine.slice(0, 30).trim() + (firstLine.length > 30 ? "..." : "") 58: const description = 59: resultContent.slice(0, 150).trim() + (resultContent.length > 150 ? "..." : "") 60: return { title, description } 61: } 62: 63: try { 64: // Get config to check for small_model 65: const config = await client.config.get() 66: const configData = config.data as { small_model?: string } | undefined 67: 68: if (!configData?.small_model) { 69: await debugLog("generateMetadata: No small_model configured, using fallback") 70: return fallbackMetadata() 71: } 72: 73: await debugLog(`generateMetadata: Using small_model ${configData.small_model}`) 74: 75: // Create a session for metadata generation 76: const session = await client.session.create({ 77: body: { 78: title: "Metadata Generation", 79: parentID, 80: }, 81: }) 82: 83: if (!session.data?.id) { 84: await debugLog("generateMetadata: Failed to create session") 85: return fallbackMetadata() 86: } 87: 88: // Prompt the small model for metadata 89: const prompt = `Generate a title and description for this research result. 90: 91: RULES: 92: - Title: 2-5 words, max 30 characters, sentence case 93: - Description: 2-3 sentences, max 150 characters, summarize key findings 94: 95: RESULT CONTENT: 96: ${resultContent.slice(0, 2000)} 97: 98: Respond with ONLY valid JSON in this exact format: 99: {"title": "Your Title Here", "description": "Your description here."}` 100: 101: // Await prompt response directly with timeout safety net 102: const PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000 103: const result = await Promise.race([ 104: client.session.prompt({ 105: path: { id: session.data.id }, 106: body: { 107: parts: [{ type: "text", text: prompt }], 108: }, 109: }), 110: new Promise<never>((_, reject) => 111: setTimeout(() => reject(new Error("Prompt timeout after 30s")), PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS), 112: ), 113: ]) 114: 115: // Extract text from the response 116: const responseParts = result.data?.parts as TextPart[] | undefined 117: const textPart = responseParts?.find((p): p is TextPart => p.type === "text") 118: if (!textPart) { 119: await debugLog("generateMetadata: No text part in response") 120: return fallbackMetadata() 121: } 122: 123: // Parse JSON response 124: const jsonMatch = textPart.text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/) 125: if (!jsonMatch) { 126: await debugLog(`generateMetadata: No JSON found in response: ${textPart.text}`) 127: return fallbackMetadata() 128: } 129: 130: const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]) as { title?: string; description?: string } 131: if (!parsed.title || !parsed.description) { 132: await debugLog("generateMetadata: Invalid JSON structure") 133: return fallbackMetadata() 134: } 135: 136: await debugLog(`generateMetadata: Generated title="${parsed.title}"`) 137: return { 138: title: parsed.title.slice(0, 30), 139: description: parsed.description.slice(0, 150), 140: } 141: } catch (error) { 142: await debugLog( 143: `generateMetadata error: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`, 144: ) 145: return fallbackMetadata() 146: } 147: } 148: 149: // ========================================== 150: // TYPE DEFINITIONS 151: // ========================================== 152: 153: interface SessionMessageItem { 154: info: Message 155: parts: Part[] 156: } 157: 158: interface AssistantSessionMessageItem { 159: info: Message & { role: "assistant" } 160: parts: Part[] 161: } 162: 163: interface DelegationProgress { 164: toolCalls: number 165: lastUpdate: Date 166: lastMessage?: string 167: lastMessageAt?: Date 168: } 169: 170: const MAX_RUN_TIME_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes 171: 172: interface Delegation { 173: id: string // Human-readable ID (e.g., "swift-amber-falcon") 174: sessionID: string 175: parentSessionID: string 176: parentMessageID: string 177: parentAgent: string 178: prompt: string 179: agent: string 180: status: "running" | "complete" | "error" | "cancelled" | "timeout" 181: startedAt: Date 182: completedAt?: Date 183: progress: DelegationProgress 184: error?: string 185: // Generated on completion by small_model 186: title?: string 187: description?: string 188: result?: string 189: } 190: 191: interface DelegateInput { 192: parentSessionID: string 193: parentMessageID: string 194: parentAgent: string 195: prompt: string 196: agent: string 197: } 198: 199: interface DelegationListItem { 200: id: string 201: status: string 202: title?: string 203: description?: string 204: agent?: string 205: } 206: 207: // ========================================== 208: // LOGGING HELPER 209: // ========================================== 210: 211: /** 212: * Create a structured logger that sends messages to OpenCode's log API. 213: * Catches errors silently to avoid disrupting tool execution. 214: */ 215: function createLogger(client: OpencodeClient) { 216: const log = (level: "debug" | "info" | "warn" | "error", message: string) => 217: client.app.log({ body: { service: "background-agents", level, message } }).catch(() => {}) 218: return { 219: debug: (msg: string) => log("debug", msg), 220: info: (msg: string) => log("info", msg), 221: warn: (msg: string) => log("warn", msg), 222: error: (msg: string) => log("error", msg), 223: } 224: } 225: 226: type Logger = ReturnType<typeof createLogger> 227: 228: // ========================================== 229: // AGENT CAPABILITY DETECTION 230: // ========================================== 231: 232: /** 233: * Parse agent mode at boundary. 234: * Returns trusted type indicating if agent is a sub-agent. 235: */ 236: async function parseAgentMode( 237: client: OpencodeClient, 238: agentName: string, 239: log: Logger, 240: ): Promise<{ isSubAgent: boolean }> { 241: try { 242: const result = await client.app.agents({}) 243: const agents = (result.data ?? []) as { name: string; mode?: string }[] 244: const agent = agents.find((a) => a.name === agentName) 245: return { isSubAgent: agent?.mode === "subagent" } 246: } catch (error) { 247: // Fail-safe: Agent list errors shouldn't block task calls 248: // Fail-loud: Log for observability 249: log.warn( 250: `Agent list fetch failed for "${agentName}", assuming non-sub-agent: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`, 251: ) 252: return { isSubAgent: false } 253: } 254: } 255: 256: /** 257: * Permission entry type: simple value or pattern object. 258: * Matches CLI schema: z.union([z.enum(["ask", "allow", "deny"]), z.record(z.enum(...))]) 259: */ 260: type PermissionEntry = "ask" | "allow" | "deny" | Record<string, "ask" | "allow" | "deny"> 261: 262: /** 263: * Check if a permission entry denies access (Law 4: Fail Fast). 264: * Handles both simple values ("deny") and pattern objects ({ "*": "deny" }). 265: */ 266: function isPermissionDenied(entry: PermissionEntry | undefined): boolean { 267: if (entry === undefined) return false 268: if (entry === "deny") return true 269: if (typeof entry === "object" && entry["*"] === "deny") return true 270: return false 271: } 272: 273: /** 274: * Parse agent write capability at boundary. 275: * Returns trusted type indicating if agent is read-only. 276: * 277: * An agent is read-only when ALL of: edit, write, and bash are denied. 278: * Permission schema supports both simple ("deny") and pattern ({ "*": "deny" }) values. 279: */ 280: async function parseAgentWriteCapability( 281: client: OpencodeClient, 282: agentName: string, 283: log: Logger, 284: ): Promise<{ isReadOnly: boolean }> { 285: try { 286: const config = await client.config.get() 287: const configData = config.data as { 288: agent?: Record< 289: string, 290: { 291: permission?: Record<string, PermissionEntry> 292: } 293: > 294: } 295: const permission = configData?.agent?.[agentName]?.permission ?? {} 296: 297: const editDenied = isPermissionDenied(permission.edit) 298: const writeDenied = isPermissionDenied(permission.write) 299: const bashDenied = isPermissionDenied(permission.bash) 300: 301: return { isReadOnly: editDenied && writeDenied && bashDenied } 302: } catch (error) { 303: // Fail-safe: Config errors shouldn't block task calls 304: // Fail-loud: Log for observability 305: log.warn( 306: `Config fetch failed for "${agentName}", assuming write-capable: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`, 307: ) 308: return { isReadOnly: false } 309: } 310: } 311: 312: /** 313: * DELEGATION MANAGER 314: */ 315: class DelegationManager { 316: private delegations: Map<string, Delegation> = new Map() 317: private client: OpencodeClient 318: private baseDir: string 319: private log: Logger 320: // Track pending delegations per parent session for batched notifications 321: private pendingByParent: Map<string, Set<string>> = new Map() 322: 323: constructor(client: OpencodeClient, baseDir: string, log: Logger) { 324: this.client = client 325: this.baseDir = baseDir 326: this.log = log 327: } 328: 329: /** 330: * Resolves the root session ID by walking up the parent chain. 331: */ 332: async getRootSessionID(sessionID: string): Promise<string> { 333: let currentID = sessionID 334: // Prevent infinite loops with max depth 335: for (let depth = 0; depth < 10; depth++) { 336: try { 337: const session = await this.client.session.get({ 338: path: { id: currentID }, 339: }) 340: 341: if (!session.data?.parentID) { 342: return currentID 343: } 344: 345: currentID = session.data.parentID 346: } catch { 347: // If we can't fetch the session, assume current is root or best effort 348: return currentID 349: } 350: } 351: return currentID 352: } 353: 354: /** 355: * Get the delegations directory for a session scope (root session) 356: */ 357: private async getDelegationsDir(sessionID: string): Promise<string> { 358: const rootID = await this.getRootSessionID(sessionID) 359: return path.join(this.baseDir, rootID) 360: } 361: 362: /** 363: * Ensure the delegations directory exists 364: */ 365: private async ensureDelegationsDir(sessionID: string): Promise<string> { 366: const dir = await this.getDelegationsDir(sessionID) 367: await fs.mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) 368: return dir 369: } 370: 371: /** 372: * Delegate a task to an agent 373: */ 374: async delegate(input: DelegateInput): Promise<Delegation> { 375: // Generate readable ID 376: const id = generateReadableId() 377: await this.debugLog(`delegate() called, generated ID: ${id}`) 378: 379: // Check for ID collisions (regenerate if needed) 380: let finalId = id 381: let attempts = 0 382: while (this.delegations.has(finalId) && attempts < 10) { 383: finalId = generateReadableId() 384: attempts++ 385: } 386: if (this.delegations.has(finalId)) { 387: throw new Error("Failed to generate unique delegation ID after 10 attempts") 388: } 389: 390: // Validate agent exists before creating session 391: const agentsResult = await this.client.app.agents({}) 392: const agents = (agentsResult.data ?? []) as { 393: name: string 394: description?: string 395: mode?: string 396: }[] 397: const validAgent = agents.find((a) => a.name === input.agent) 398: 399: if (!validAgent) { 400: const available = agents 401: .filter((a) => a.mode === "subagent" || a.mode === "all" || !a.mode) 402: .map((a) => `• ${a.name}${a.description ? ` - ${a.description}` : ""}`) 403: .join("\n") 404: 405: throw new Error( 406: `Agent "${input.agent}" not found.\n\nAvailable agents:\n${available || "(none)"}`, 407: ) 408: } 409: 410: // Check if agent is read-only (Early Exit + Fail Fast) 411: const { isReadOnly } = await parseAgentWriteCapability(this.client, input.agent, this.log) 412: if (!isReadOnly) { 413: throw new Error( 414: `Agent "${input.agent}" is write-capable and requires the native \`task\` tool for proper undo/branching support.\n\n` + 415: `Use \`task\` instead of \`delegate\` for write-capable agents.\n\n` + 416: `Read-only agents (researcher, explore) use \`delegate\`.\n` + 417: `Write-capable agents (coder, scribe) use \`task\`.`, 418: ) 419: } 420: 421: // Create isolated session for delegation 422: const sessionResult = await this.client.session.create({ 423: body: { 424: title: `Delegation: ${finalId}`, 425: parentID: input.parentSessionID, 426: }, 427: }) 428: 429: await this.debugLog(`session.create result: ${JSON.stringify(sessionResult.data)}`) 430: 431: if (!sessionResult.data?.id) { 432: throw new Error("Failed to create delegation session") 433: } 434: 435: const delegation: Delegation = { 436: id: finalId, 437: sessionID: sessionResult.data.id, 438: parentSessionID: input.parentSessionID, 439: parentMessageID: input.parentMessageID, 440: parentAgent: input.parentAgent, 441: prompt: input.prompt, 442: agent: input.agent, 443: status: "running", 444: startedAt: new Date(), 445: progress: { 446: toolCalls: 0, 447: lastUpdate: new Date(), 448: }, 449: } 450: 451: await this.debugLog(`Created delegation ${delegation.id}`) 452: this.delegations.set(delegation.id, delegation) 453: 454: // Track this delegation for batched notification 455: const parentId = input.parentSessionID 456: if (!this.pendingByParent.has(parentId)) { 457: this.pendingByParent.set(parentId, new Set()) 458: } 459: this.pendingByParent.get(parentId)?.add(delegation.id) 460: await this.debugLog( 461: `Tracking delegation ${delegation.id} for parent ${parentId}. Pending count: ${this.pendingByParent.get(parentId)?.size}`, 462: ) 463: 464: await this.debugLog( 465: `Delegation added to map. Current delegations: ${Array.from(this.delegations.keys()).join(", ")}`, 466: ) 467: 468: // Set a timer for the global max run time 469: setTimeout(() => { 470: const current = this.delegations.get(delegation.id) 471: if (current && current.status === "running") { 472: this.handleTimeout(delegation.id) 473: } 474: }, MAX_RUN_TIME_MS + 5000) // Adding 5s buffer 475: 476: // Ensure delegations directory exists (early check) 477: await this.ensureDelegationsDir(input.parentSessionID) 478: 479: // Fire the prompt (using prompt() instead of promptAsync() to properly initialize agent loop) 480: // Agent param is critical for MCP tools - tells OpenCode which agent's config to use 481: // Anti-recursion: disable nested delegations and state-modifying tools via tools config 482: this.client.session 483: .prompt({ 484: path: { id: delegation.sessionID }, 485: body: { 486: agent: input.agent, 487: parts: [{ type: "text", text: input.prompt }], 488: tools: { 489: task: false, 490: delegate: false, 491: todowrite: false, 492: plan_save: false, 493: }, 494: }, 495: }) 496: .catch((error: Error) => { 497: delegation.status = "error" 498: delegation.error = error.message 499: delegation.completedAt = new Date() 500: this.persistOutput(delegation, `Error: ${error.message}`) 501: this.notifyParent(delegation) 502: }) 503: 504: return delegation 505: } 506: 507: /** 508: * Handle delegation timeout 509: */ 510: private async handleTimeout(delegationId: string): Promise<void> { 511: const delegation = this.delegations.get(delegationId) 512: if (!delegation || delegation.status !== "running") return 513: 514: await this.debugLog(`handleTimeout for delegation ${delegation.id}`) 515: 516: delegation.status = "timeout" 517: delegation.completedAt = new Date() 518: delegation.error = `Delegation timed out after ${MAX_RUN_TIME_MS / 1000}s` 519: 520: // Try to cancel the session 521: try { 522: await this.client.session.delete({ 523: path: { id: delegation.sessionID }, 524: }) 525: } catch { 526: // Ignore 527: } 528: 529: // Get whatever result was produced so far 530: const result = await this.getResult(delegation) 531: await this.persistOutput(delegation, `${result}\n\n[TIMEOUT REACHED]`) 532: 533: // Notify parent session 534: await this.notifyParent(delegation) 535: } 536: 537: /** 538: * Wait for a delegation to complete (polling) 539: */ 540: private async waitForCompletion(delegationId: string): Promise<void> { 541: const pollInterval = 1000 542: const startTime = Date.now() 543: 544: const delegation = this.delegations.get(delegationId) 545: if (!delegation) return 546: 547: while ( 548: delegation.status === "running" && 549: Date.now() - startTime < MAX_RUN_TIME_MS + 10000 // Slightly more than global limit 550: ) { 551: await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, pollInterval)) 552: } 553: } 554: 555: /** 556: * Handle session.idle event - called when a session becomes idle 557: */ 558: async handleSessionIdle(sessionID: string): Promise<void> { 559: const delegation = this.findBySession(sessionID) 560: if (!delegation || delegation.status !== "running") return 561: 562: await this.debugLog(`handleSessionIdle for delegation ${delegation.id}`) 563: 564: delegation.status = "complete" 565: delegation.completedAt = new Date() 566: 567: // Get the result 568: const result = await this.getResult(delegation) 569: delegation.result = result 570: 571: // Generate title and description using small model 572: const metadata = await generateMetadata(this.client, result, delegation.sessionID, (msg) => 573: this.debugLog(msg), 574: ) 575: delegation.title = metadata.title 576: delegation.description = metadata.description 577: 578: // Persist output with generated metadata 579: await this.persistOutput(delegation, result) 580: 581: // Notify parent session 582: await this.notifyParent(delegation) 583: } 584: 585: /** 586: * Get the result from a delegation's session 587: */ 588: private async getResult(delegation: Delegation): Promise<string> { 589: try { 590: const messages = await this.client.session.messages({ 591: path: { id: delegation.sessionID }, 592: }) 593: 594: const messageData = messages.data as SessionMessageItem[] | undefined 595: 596: if (!messageData || messageData.length === 0) { 597: await this.debugLog(`getResult: No messages found for session ${delegation.sessionID}`) 598: return `Delegation "${delegation.description}" completed but produced no output.` 599: } 600: 601: await this.debugLog( 602: `getResult: Found ${messageData.length} messages. Roles: ${messageData.map((m) => m.info.role).join(", ")}`, 603: ) 604: 605: // Find the last message from the assistant/model 606: const isAssistantMessage = (m: SessionMessageItem): m is AssistantSessionMessageItem => 607: m.info.role === "assistant" 608: 609: const assistantMessages = messageData.filter(isAssistantMessage) 610: 611: if (assistantMessages.length === 0) { 612: await this.debugLog( 613: `getResult: No assistant messages found in ${JSON.stringify(messageData.map((m) => ({ role: m.info.role, keys: Object.keys(m) })))}`, 614: ) 615: return `Delegation "${delegation.description}" completed but produced no assistant response.` 616: } 617: 618: const lastMessage = assistantMessages[assistantMessages.length - 1] 619: 620: // Extract text parts from the message 621: const isTextPart = (p: Part): p is TextPart => p.type === "text" 622: const textParts = lastMessage.parts.filter(isTextPart) 623: 624: if (textParts.length === 0) { 625: await this.debugLog( 626: `getResult: No text parts found in message: ${JSON.stringify(lastMessage)}`, 627: ) 628: return `Delegation "${delegation.description}" completed but produced no text content.` 629: } 630: 631: return textParts.map((p) => p.text).join("\n") 632: } catch (error) { 633: await this.debugLog( 634: `getResult error: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`, 635: ) 636: return `Delegation "${delegation.description}" completed but result could not be retrieved: ${ 637: error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error" 638: }` 639: } 640: } 641: 642: /** 643: * Persist delegation output to storage 644: */ 645: private async persistOutput(delegation: Delegation, content: string): Promise<void> { 646: try { 647: // Ensure we resolve the root session ID of the PARENT session for storage 648: const dir = await this.ensureDelegationsDir(delegation.parentSessionID) 649: const filePath = path.join(dir, `${delegation.id}.md`) 650: 651: // Use title/description if available (generated by small model), otherwise fallback 652: const title = delegation.title || delegation.id 653: const description = delegation.description || "(No description generated)" 654: 655: const header = `# ${title} 656: 657: ${description} 658: 659: **ID:** ${delegation.id} 660: **Agent:** ${delegation.agent} 661: **Status:** ${delegation.status} 662: **Started:** ${delegation.startedAt.toISOString()} 663: **Completed:** ${delegation.completedAt?.toISOString() || "N/A"} 664: 665: --- 666: 667: ` 668: await fs.writeFile(filePath, header + content, "utf8") 669: await this.debugLog(`Persisted output to ${filePath}`) 670: } catch (error) { 671: await this.debugLog( 672: `Failed to persist output: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`, 673: ) 674: } 675: } 676: 677: /** 678: * Notify parent session that delegation is complete. 679: * Uses batching: individual notifications are silent (noReply: true), 680: * but when ALL delegations for a parent session complete, triggers a response. 681: */ 682: private async notifyParent(delegation: Delegation): Promise<void> { 683: try { 684: // Use generated title/description if available 685: const title = delegation.title || delegation.id 686: const statusText = delegation.status === "complete" ? "complete" : delegation.status 687: const result = delegation.result || "(No result)" 688: 689: // Mark this delegation as complete in the pending tracker 690: const pendingSet = this.pendingByParent.get(delegation.parentSessionID) 691: if (pendingSet) { 692: pendingSet.delete(delegation.id) 693: } 694: 695: // Check if ALL delegations for this parent are now complete 696: const allComplete = !pendingSet || pendingSet.size === 0 697: 698: // Clean up if all complete 699: if (allComplete && pendingSet) { 700: this.pendingByParent.delete(delegation.parentSessionID) 701: } 702: 703: const remainingCount = pendingSet?.size || 0 704: 705: // Always send the completed delegation notification first 706: const progressNote = 707: remainingCount > 0 708: ? ` 709: **${remainingCount} delegation${remainingCount === 1 ? "" : "s"} still in progress.** You WILL be notified when ALL complete. 710: ❌ Do NOT poll \`delegation_list\` - continue productive work.` 711: : "" 712: const completionNotification = `<task-notification> 713: <task-id>${delegation.id}</task-id> 714: <status>${statusText}</status> 715: <summary>Agent "${title}" ${statusText}</summary> 716: <result>${result}</result> 717: ${delegation.error ? `\n<error>${delegation.error}</error>` : ""} 718: </task-notification>${progressNote}` 719: 720: await this.client.session.prompt({ 721: path: { id: delegation.parentSessionID }, 722: body: { 723: noReply: true, 724: agent: delegation.parentAgent, 725: parts: [{ type: "text", text: completionNotification }], 726: }, 727: }) 728: 729: // If all delegations complete, send a minimal completion notice that triggers response 730: if (allComplete) { 731: const allCompleteNotification = `<task-notification> 732: <status>completed</status> 733: <summary>All delegations complete.</summary> 734: </task-notification>` 735: 736: await this.client.session.prompt({ 737: path: { id: delegation.parentSessionID }, 738: body: { 739: noReply: false, 740: agent: delegation.parentAgent, 741: parts: [{ type: "text", text: allCompleteNotification }], 742: }, 743: }) 744: } 745: 746: await this.debugLog( 747: `Notified parent session ${delegation.parentSessionID} (allComplete=${allComplete}, remaining=${pendingSet?.size || 0})`, 748: ) 749: } catch (error) { 750: await this.debugLog( 751: `Failed to notify parent: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`, 752: ) 753: } 754: } 755: 756: /** 757: * Read a delegation's output by ID. Blocks if the delegation is still running. 758: */ 759: async readOutput(sessionID: string, id: string): Promise<string> { 760: // Try to find the file 761: let filePath: string | undefined 762: try { 763: const dir = await this.getDelegationsDir(sessionID) 764: filePath = path.join(dir, `${id}.md`) 765: // Check if file exists 766: await fs.access(filePath) 767: return await fs.readFile(filePath, "utf8") 768: } catch { 769: // File doesn't exist yet, continue to check memory 770: } 771: 772: // Check if it's currently running in memory 773: const delegation = this.delegations.get(id) 774: if (delegation) { 775: if (delegation.status === "running") { 776: await this.debugLog(`readOutput: waiting for delegation ${delegation.id} to complete`) 777: await this.waitForCompletion(delegation.id) 778: 779: // Re-check after waiting 780: const dir = await this.getDelegationsDir(sessionID) 781: filePath = path.join(dir, `${id}.md`) 782: try { 783: return await fs.readFile(filePath, "utf8") 784: } catch { 785: // Still failed to read 786: } 787: 788: // If still no file after waiting (e.g. error/timeout/cancel) 789: const updated = this.delegations.get(id) 790: if (updated && updated.status !== "running") { 791: const title = updated.title || updated.id 792: return `Delegation "${title}" ended with status: ${updated.status}. ${updated.error || ""}` 793: } 794: } 795: } 796: 797: throw new Error( 798: `Delegation "${id}" not found.\n\nUse delegation_list() to see available delegations.`, 799: ) 800: } 801: 802: /** 803: * List all delegations for a session 804: */ 805: async listDelegations(sessionID: string): Promise<DelegationListItem[]> { 806: const results: DelegationListItem[] = [] 807: 808: // Add in-memory delegations that match this session (or parent) 809: for (const delegation of this.delegations.values()) { 810: results.push({ 811: id: delegation.id, 812: status: delegation.status, 813: title: delegation.title || "(generating...)", 814: description: delegation.description || "(generating...)", 815: }) 816: } 817: 818: // Check filesystem for persisted delegations 819: try { 820: const dir = await this.getDelegationsDir(sessionID) 821: const files = await fs.readdir(dir) 822: 823: for (const file of files) { 824: if (file.endsWith(".md")) { 825: const id = file.replace(".md", "") 826: // Deduplicate: prioritize in-memory status 827: if (!results.find((r) => r.id === id)) { 828: // Try to read title, agent, description from file 829: let title = "(loaded from storage)" 830: let description = "" 831: let agent: string | undefined 832: try { 833: const filePath = path.join(dir, file) 834: const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, "utf8") 835: const titleMatch = content.match(/^# (.+)$/m) 836: if (titleMatch) title = titleMatch[1] 837: const agentMatch = content.match(/^\*\*Agent:\*\* (.+)$/m) 838: if (agentMatch) agent = agentMatch[1] 839: // Get first paragraph after title as description 840: const lines = content.split("\n") 841: if (lines.length > 2 && lines[2]) { 842: description = lines[2].slice(0, 150) 843: } 844: } catch { 845: // Ignore read errors 846: } 847: results.push({ 848: id, 849: status: "complete", 850: title, 851: description, 852: agent, 853: }) 854: } 855: } 856: } 857: } catch { 858: // Directory may not exist yet 859: } 860: 861: return results 862: } 863: 864: /** 865: * Delete a delegation by id (cancels if running, removes from storage) 866: * Used internally for cleanup (timeout, etc.) 867: */ 868: async deleteDelegation(sessionID: string, id: string): Promise<boolean> { 869: // Find delegation by id 870: let delegationId: string | undefined 871: for (const [dId, d] of this.delegations) { 872: if (d.id === id) { 873: delegationId = dId 874: break 875: } 876: } 877: 878: if (delegationId) { 879: const delegation = this.delegations.get(delegationId) 880: if (delegation?.status === "running") { 881: try { 882: await this.client.session.delete({ 883: path: { id: delegation.sessionID }, 884: }) 885: } catch { 886: // Session may already be deleted 887: } 888: delegation.status = "cancelled" 889: delegation.completedAt = new Date() 890: } 891: this.delegations.delete(delegationId) 892: } 893: 894: // Remove from filesystem 895: try { 896: const dir = await this.getDelegationsDir(sessionID) 897: const filePath = path.join(dir, `${id}.md`) 898: await fs.unlink(filePath) 899: return true 900: } catch { 901: return false 902: } 903: } 904: 905: /** 906: * Find a delegation by its session ID 907: */ 908: findBySession(sessionID: string): Delegation | undefined { 909: return Array.from(this.delegations.values()).find((d) => d.sessionID === sessionID) 910: } 911: 912: /** 913: * Handle message events for progress tracking 914: */ 915: handleMessageEvent(sessionID: string, messageText?: string): void { 916: const delegation = this.findBySession(sessionID) 917: if (!delegation || delegation.status !== "running") return 918: 919: delegation.progress.lastUpdate = new Date() 920: if (messageText) { 921: delegation.progress.lastMessage = messageText 922: delegation.progress.lastMessageAt = new Date() 923: } 924: } 925: 926: /** 927: * Get count of pending delegations for a parent session 928: */ 929: getPendingCount(parentSessionID: string): number { 930: const pendingSet = this.pendingByParent.get(parentSessionID) 931: return pendingSet ? pendingSet.size : 0 932: } 933: 934: /** 935: * Get all currently running delegations (in-memory only) 936: */ 937: getRunningDelegations(): Delegation[] { 938: return Array.from(this.delegations.values()).filter((d) => d.status === "running") 939: } 940: 941: /** 942: * Get recent completed delegations for compaction injection 943: */ 944: async getRecentCompletedDelegations( 945: sessionID: string, 946: limit: number = 10, 947: ): Promise<DelegationListItem[]> { 948: const all = await this.listDelegations(sessionID) 949: return all.filter((d) => d.status !== "running").slice(-limit) 950: } 951: 952: /** 953: * Log debug messages 954: */ 955: async debugLog(msg: string): Promise<void> { 956: // Only log if debug is enabled (could be env var or static const) 957: // For now, mirroring previous behavior but writing to the new baseDir/debug.log 958: const timestamp = new Date().toISOString() 959: const line = `${timestamp}: ${msg}\n` 960: const debugFile = path.join(this.baseDir, "background-agents-debug.log") 961: 962: try { 963: await fs.appendFile(debugFile, line, "utf8") 964: } catch { 965: // Ignore errors, try to ensure dir once if it fails? 966: // Simpler to just ignore for debug logs 967: } 968: } 969: } 970: 971: // ========================================== 972: // TOOL CREATORS 973: // ========================================== 974: 975: interface DelegateArgs { 976: prompt: string 977: agent: string 978: } 979: 980: function createDelegate(manager: DelegationManager): ReturnType<typeof tool> { 981: return tool({ 982: description: `Delegate a task to an agent. Returns immediately with a readable ID. 983: 984: Use this for: 985: - Research tasks (will be auto-saved) 986: - Parallel work that can run in background 987: - Any task where you want persistent, retrievable output 988: 989: On completion, a notification will arrive with the ID, title, description, and result. 990: Use \`delegation_read\` with the ID to retrieve the result again if it is lost during compaction.`, 991: args: { 992: prompt: tool.schema 993: .string() 994: .describe("The full detailed prompt for the agent. Must be in English."), 995: agent: tool.schema 996: .string() 997: .describe( 998: 'Agent to delegate to: "explore" (codebase search), "researcher" (external research), "scribe" (docs/commits), or "general".', 999: ), 1000: }, 1001: async execute(args: DelegateArgs, toolCtx: ToolContext): Promise<string> { 1002: if (!toolCtx?.sessionID) { 1003: return "❌ delegate requires sessionID. This is a system error." 1004: } 1005: if (!toolCtx?.messageID) { 1006: return "❌ delegate requires messageID. This is a system error." 1007: } 1008: 1009: try { 1010: const delegation = await manager.delegate({ 1011: parentSessionID: toolCtx.sessionID, 1012: parentMessageID: toolCtx.messageID, 1013: parentAgent: toolCtx.agent, 1014: prompt: args.prompt, 1015: agent: args.agent, 1016: }) 1017: 1018: // Get total active count for this parent session 1019: const pendingSet = manager.getPendingCount(toolCtx.sessionID) 1020: const totalActive = pendingSet 1021: 1022: let response = `Delegation started: ${delegation.id}\nAgent: ${args.agent}` 1023: if (totalActive > 1) { 1024: response += `\n\n${totalActive} delegations now active.` 1025: } 1026: response += `\nYou WILL be notified when ${totalActive > 1 ? "ALL complete" : "complete"}. Do NOT poll.` 1027: 1028: return response 1029: } catch (error) { 1030: // Return validation errors as guidance, not exceptions 1031: return `❌ Delegation failed:\n\n${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}` 1032: } 1033: }, 1034: }) 1035: } 1036: 1037: function createDelegationRead(manager: DelegationManager): ReturnType<typeof tool> { 1038: return tool({ 1039: description: `Read the output of a delegation by its ID. 1040: Use this to retrieve results from delegated tasks if the inline notification was lost during compaction.`, 1041: args: { 1042: id: tool.schema.string().describe("The delegation ID (e.g., 'elegant-blue-tiger')"), 1043: }, 1044: async execute(args: { id: string }, toolCtx: ToolContext): Promise<string> { 1045: if (!toolCtx?.sessionID) { 1046: return "❌ delegation_read requires sessionID. This is a system error." 1047: } 1048: 1049: return await manager.readOutput(toolCtx.sessionID, args.id) 1050: }, 1051: }) 1052: } 1053: 1054: function createDelegationList(manager: DelegationManager): ReturnType<typeof tool> { 1055: return tool({ 1056: description: `List all delegations for the current session. 1057: Shows both running and completed delegations.`, 1058: args: {}, 1059: async execute(_args: Record<string, never>, toolCtx: ToolContext): Promise<string> { 1060: if (!toolCtx?.sessionID) { 1061: return "❌ delegation_list requires sessionID. This is a system error." 1062: } 1063: 1064: const delegations = await manager.listDelegations(toolCtx.sessionID) 1065: 1066: if (delegations.length === 0) { 1067: return "No delegations found for this session." 1068: } 1069: 1070: const lines = delegations.map((d) => { 1071: const titlePart = d.title ? ` | ${d.title}` : "" 1072: const descPart = d.description ? `\n → ${d.description}` : "" 1073: return `- **${d.id}**${titlePart} [${d.status}]${descPart}` 1074: }) 1075: 1076: return `## Delegations\n\n${lines.join("\n")}` 1077: }, 1078: }) 1079: } 1080: 1081: // ========================================== 1082: // DELEGATION RULES (injected into system prompt) 1083: // ========================================== 1084: 1085: const DELEGATION_RULES = `<task-notification> 1086: <delegation-system> 1087: 1088: ## Async Delegation 1089: 1090: You have tools for parallel background work: 1091: - \`delegate(prompt, agent)\` - Launch task, returns ID immediately 1092: - \`delegation_read(id)\` - Retrieve completed result 1093: - \`delegation_list()\` - List delegations (use sparingly) 1094: 1095: ## Delegation Routing 1096: 1097: Agents route based on their permissions: 1098: 1099: | Agent Type | Tool | Why | 1100: |------------|------|-----| 1101: | Read-only (researcher, explore) | \`delegate\` | Background session, async | 1102: | Write-capable (coder, scribe) | \`task\` | Native task, preserves undo/branching | 1103: 1104: **Read-only agents** have edit="deny", write="deny", bash={"*":"deny"}. 1105: **Write-capable agents** have any write tool enabled. 1106: 1107: ## How It Works 1108: 1109: 1. For read-only agents: Call \`delegate\` with detailed prompt 1110: 2. For write-capable agents: Call \`task\` with detailed prompt 1111: 3. Continue productive work while it runs 1112: 4. Receive notification when complete 1113: 5. Call \`delegation_read(id)\` to retrieve results 1114: 1115: ## Critical Constraints 1116: 1117: **NEVER poll \`delegation_list\` to check completion.** 1118: You WILL be notified via \`<task-notification>\`. Polling wastes tokens. 1119: 1120: **NEVER wait idle.** Always have productive work while delegations run. 1121: 1122: **Using wrong tool will fail fast with guidance.** 1123: 1124: </delegation-system> 1125: </task-notification>` 1126: 1127: // ========================================== 1128: // COMPACTION CONTEXT FORMATTING 1129: // ========================================== 1130: 1131: interface DelegationForContext { 1132: id: string 1133: agent?: string 1134: title?: string 1135: description?: string 1136: status: string 1137: startedAt?: Date 1138: prompt?: string 1139: } 1140: 1141: /** 1142: * Format delegation context for injection during compaction. 1143: * Includes running delegations with notification reminder (only when running exist), 1144: * and recent completed delegations with full descriptions. 1145: */ 1146: function formatDelegationContext( 1147: running: DelegationForContext[], 1148: completed: DelegationForContext[], 1149: ): string { 1150: const sections: string[] = ["<delegation-context>"] 1151: 1152: // Running delegations (if any) 1153: if (running.length > 0) { 1154: sections.push("## Running Delegations") 1155: sections.push("") 1156: for (const d of running) { 1157: sections.push(`### \`${d.id}\`${d.agent ? ` (${d.agent})` : ""}`) 1158: if (d.startedAt) { 1159: sections.push(`**Started:** ${d.startedAt.toISOString()}`) 1160: } 1161: if (d.prompt) { 1162: const truncatedPrompt = d.prompt.length > 200 ? `${d.prompt.slice(0, 200)}...` : d.prompt 1163: sections.push(`**Prompt:** ${truncatedPrompt}`) 1164: } 1165: sections.push("") 1166: } 1167: 1168: // Only include reminder when there ARE running delegations 1169: sections.push( 1170: "> **Note:** You WILL be notified via `<task-notification>` when delegations complete.", 1171: ) 1172: sections.push("> Do NOT poll `delegation_list` - continue productive work.") 1173: sections.push("") 1174: } 1175: 1176: // Completed delegations (recent) 1177: if (completed.length > 0) { 1178: sections.push("## Recent Completed Delegations") 1179: sections.push("") 1180: for (const d of completed) { 1181: const statusEmoji = 1182: d.status === "complete" 1183: ? "✅" 1184: : d.status === "error" 1185: ? "❌" 1186: : d.status === "timeout" 1187: ? "⏱️" 1188: : "🚫" 1189: sections.push(`### ${statusEmoji} \`${d.id}\``) 1190: sections.push(`**Title:** ${d.title || "(no title)"}`) 1191: sections.push(`**Status:** ${d.status}`) 1192: sections.push(`**Description:** ${d.description || "(no description)"}`) 1193: sections.push("") 1194: } 1195: sections.push("> Use `delegation_list()` to see all delegations for this session.") 1196: sections.push("") 1197: } 1198: 1199: sections.push("## Retrieval") 1200: sections.push('Use `delegation_read("id")` to access full delegation output.') 1201: sections.push("</delegation-context>") 1202: 1203: return sections.join("\n") 1204: } 1205: 1206: // ========================================== 1207: // PLUGIN EXPORT 1208: // ========================================== 1209: 1210: /** 1211: * Expected input for experimental.chat.system.transform hook. 1212: */ 1213: interface SystemTransformInput { 1214: agent?: string 1215: sessionID?: string 1216: } 1217: 1218: export const BackgroundAgentsPlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => { 1219: const { client, directory } = ctx 1220: 1221: // Create logger early for all components 1222: const log = createLogger(client as OpencodeClient) 1223: 1224: // Project-level storage directory (shared across sessions) 1225: // Uses git root commit hash for cross-worktree consistency 1226: const projectId = await getProjectId(directory) 1227: const baseDir = path.join(os.homedir(), ".local", "share", "opencode", "delegations", projectId) 1228: 1229: // Ensure base directory exists (for debug logs etc) 1230: await fs.mkdir(baseDir, { recursive: true }) 1231: 1232: const manager = new DelegationManager(client as OpencodeClient, baseDir, log) 1233: 1234: await manager.debugLog("BackgroundAgentsPlugin initialized with delegation system") 1235: 1236: return { 1237: tool: { 1238: delegate: createDelegate(manager), 1239: delegation_read: createDelegationRead(manager), 1240: delegation_list: createDelegationList(manager), 1241: }, 1242: 1243: // Prevent read-only agents from using native task tool (symmetric to delegate enforcement) 1244: "tool.execute.before": async ( 1245: input: { tool: string }, 1246: output: { args?: { subagent_type?: string } }, 1247: ) => { 1248: // Guard: Only intercept task tool 1249: if (input.tool !== "task") return 1250: 1251: // Guard: Require agent name 1252: const agentName = output.args?.subagent_type 1253: if (!agentName) return 1254: 1255: // Parse boundary 1: Check agent mode 1256: const { isSubAgent } = await parseAgentMode(client as OpencodeClient, agentName, log) 1257: 1258: // Guard: Allow non-sub-agents (main/built-in) 1259: if (!isSubAgent) return 1260: 1261: // Parse boundary 2: Check write capability (only for sub-agents) 1262: const { isReadOnly } = await parseAgentWriteCapability( 1263: client as OpencodeClient, 1264: agentName, 1265: log, 1266: ) 1267: 1268: // Guard: Allow write-capable agents 1269: if (!isReadOnly) return 1270: 1271: // Fail fast: Read-only sub-agent via task is invalid 1272: throw new Error( 1273: `❌ Agent '${agentName}' is read-only and should use the delegate tool for async background execution.\n\n` + 1274: `Read-only agents have: edit="deny", write="deny", bash={"*":"deny"}\n` + 1275: `Use delegate for: researcher, explore\n` + 1276: `Use task for: coder, scribe`, 1277: ) 1278: }, 1279: 1280: // Inject delegation rules into system prompt 1281: "experimental.chat.system.transform": async (_input: SystemTransformInput, output) => { 1282: output.system.push(DELEGATION_RULES) 1283: }, 1284: 1285: // Compaction hook - inject delegation context for context recovery 1286: "experimental.session.compacting": async ( 1287: input: { sessionID: string }, 1288: output: { context: string[]; prompt?: string }, 1289: ) => { 1290: const rootSessionID = await manager.getRootSessionID(input.sessionID) 1291: 1292: // Get running delegations for this session tree 1293: const running = manager 1294: .getRunningDelegations() 1295: .filter((d) => d.parentSessionID === input.sessionID || d.parentSessionID === rootSessionID) 1296: .map((d) => ({ 1297: id: d.id, 1298: agent: d.agent, 1299: title: d.title, 1300: description: d.description, 1301: status: d.status, 1302: startedAt: d.startedAt, 1303: prompt: d.prompt, 1304: })) 1305: 1306: // Get recent completed delegations (last 10) 1307: const allDelegations = await manager.listDelegations(input.sessionID) 1308: const completed = allDelegations 1309: .filter((d) => d.status !== "running") 1310: .slice(-10) 1311: .map((d) => ({ 1312: id: d.id, 1313: agent: d.agent, 1314: title: d.title, 1315: description: d.description, 1316: status: d.status, 1317: })) 1318: 1319: // Early exit if nothing to inject 1320: if (running.length === 0 && completed.length === 0) return 1321: 1322: output.context.push(formatDelegationContext(running, completed)) 1323: }, 1324: 1325: // Event hook 1326: event: async ({ event }: { event: Event }): Promise<void> => { 1327: if (event.type === "session.idle") { 1328: const sessionID = event.properties.sessionID 1329: const delegation = manager.findBySession(sessionID) 1330: if (delegation) { 1331: await manager.handleSessionIdle(sessionID) 1332: } 1333: } 1334: 1335: if (event.type === "message.updated") { 1336: const sessionID = event.properties.info.sessionID 1337: if (sessionID) { 1338: manager.handleMessageEvent(sessionID) 1339: } 1340: } 1341: }, 1342: } 1343: } 1344: 1345: export default BackgroundAgentsPlugin 1: /** 2: * notify 3: * Native OS notifications for OpenCode 4: * 5: * Philosophy: "Notify the human when the AI needs them back, not for every micro-event." 6: * 7: * Features: 8: * - Uses cmux native notifications when running inside cmux 9: * - Auto-detects terminal emulator (Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm, WezTerm, etc.) 10: * - Suppresses notifications when terminal is focused (like Ghostty does) 11: * - Click notification to focus terminal 12: * - Parent session only by default (no spam from sub-tasks) 13: * 14: * Uses cmux CLI first (if available), then node-notifier fallback: 15: * - cmux: `cmux notify --title ... --subtitle ... --body ...` 16: * - macOS: terminal-notifier (native NSUserNotificationCenter) 17: * - Windows: SnoreToast (native toast notifications) 18: * - Linux: notify-send (native desktop notifications) 19: */ 20: 21: import * as fs from "node:fs/promises" 22: import * as os from "node:os" 23: import * as path from "node:path" 24: import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin" 25: import type { Event } from "@opencode-ai/sdk" 26: // @ts-expect-error - installed at runtime by OCX 27: import detectTerminal from "detect-terminal" 28: // @ts-expect-error - installed at runtime by OCX 29: import notifier from "node-notifier" 30: import type { OpencodeClient } from "./kdco-primitives/types" 31: import { sendNotificationWithFallback } from "./notify/backend" 32: import { canUseCmuxNotification, sendCmuxNotification } from "./notify/cmux" 33: 34: interface NotifyConfig { 35: /** Notify for child/sub-session events (default: false) */ 36: notifyChildSessions: boolean 37: /** Sound configuration per event type */ 38: sounds: { 39: idle: string 40: error: string 41: permission: string 42: question?: string 43: } 44: /** Quiet hours configuration */ 45: quietHours: { 46: enabled: boolean 47: start: string // "HH:MM" 118: } 119: 120: // ========================================== 121: // TERMINAL DETECTION (macOS) 122: // ========================================== 123: 124: async function runOsascript(script: string): Promise<string | null> { 125: if (process.platform !== "darwin") return null 126: 127: try { 128: const proc = Bun.spawn(["osascript", "-e", script], { 129: stdout: "pipe", 130: stderr: "pipe", 131: }) 132: const output = await new Response(proc.stdout).text() 133: return output.trim() 134: } catch { 135: return null 136: } 137: } 138: 139: async function getBundleId(appName: string): Promise<string | null> { 140: return runOsascript(`id of application "${appName}"`) 141: } 142: 143: async function getFrontmostApp(): Promise<string | null> { 144: return runOsascript( 145: 'tell application "System Events" to get name of first application process whose frontmost is true', 146: ) 147: } 148: 149: async function detectTerminalInfo(config: NotifyConfig): null, processName: null } 155: } 156: 157: // Get process name for focus detection 158: const processName = TERMINAL_PROCESS_NAMES[terminalName.toLowerCase()] || terminalName 159: 160: // Dynamically get bundle ID from macOS (no hardcoding!) 161: const bundleId = await getBundleId(processName) 162: 163: return { 164: name: terminalName, 165: bundleId, 166: processName, 167: } 168: } 169: 170: async function isTerminalFocused(terminalInfo: TerminalInfo): Promise<boolean> { 171: if (!terminalInfo.processName) return false 172: if (process.platform !== "darwin") return false 173: 174: const frontmost = await getFrontmostApp() 175: if (!frontmost) return false 176: 177: // Case-insensitive comparison 178: return frontmost.toLowerCase() === terminalInfo.processName.toLowerCase() 179: } 180: 181: // ========================================== 182: // QUIET HOURS CHECK 183: // ========================================== 184: 185: function isQuietHours(config: NotifyConfig): boolean { 186: if (!config.quietHours.enabled) return false 187: 188: const now = new Date() 189: const currentMinutes = now.getHours() * 60 + now.getMinutes() 190: 191: const [startHour, startMin] = config.quietHours.start.split(":").map(Number) 192: const [endHour, endMin] = config.quietHours.end.split(":").map(Number) 193: 194: const startMinutes = startHour * 60 + startMin 195: const endMinutes = endHour * 60 + endMin 196: 197: // Handle overnight quiet hours (e.g., 22:00 - 08:00) 198: if (startMinutes > endMinutes) { 199: return currentMinutes >= startMinutes || currentMinutes < endMinutes 200: } 201: 202: return currentMinutes >= startMinutes && currentMinutes < endMinutes 203: } 204: 205: // ========================================== 206: // PARENT SESSION DETECTION 207: // ========================================== 208: 209: async function isParentSession(client: OpencodeClient, sessionID: string): Promise<boolean> { 210: try { 211: const session = await client.session.get({ path: { id: sessionID } }) 212: // No parentID means this IS the parent/root session 213: return !session.data?.parentID 214: } catch { 215: // If we can't fetch, assume it's a parent to be safe (notify rather than miss) 216: return true 217: } 218: } 219: 220: // ========================================== 221: // NOTIFICATION SENDER 222: // "string") return null 245: 246: const normalized = value.trim() 247: if (!normalized) return null 248: 249: return normalized 250: } 251: 252: function shouldSendDedupedNotification( 253: recentNotifications: RecentNotifications, 254: dedupeKey: string, 255: windowMs: number, 256: nowMs = Date.now(), 257: ): boolean { 258: for (const [key, timestamp] of recentNotifications) { 259: if (nowMs - timestamp >= windowMs) { 260: recentNotifications.delete(key) 261: } 262: } 263: 264: const lastSentAt = recentNotifications.get(dedupeKey) 265: if (lastSentAt !== undefined && nowMs - lastSentAt < windowMs) { 266: return false 267: } 268: 269: recentNotifications.set(dedupeKey, nowMs) 270: return true 271: } 272: 273: function buildQuestionToolDedupeKey(sessionID: unknown, callID: sessionID: string, 365: config: NotifyConfig, 366: terminalInfo: TerminalInfo, 367: notificationRuntime: NotificationRuntime, 368: ): Promise<void> { 369: // Check if we should notify for this session 370: if (!config.notifyChildSessions) { 371: const isParent = await isParentSession(client, sessionID) 372: if (!isParent) return 373: } 374: 375: // Check quiet hours 376: if (isQuietHours(config)) 387: } 388: } catch { 389: // Use default title 390: } 391: 392: await sendNotification( 393: { 394: title: "Ready for review", 395: message: sessionTitle, 396: subtitle: sessionTitle, 397: cmuxBody: "OpenCode task is ready for review", 398: sound: config.sounds.idle, 399: terminalInfo, 400: }, 401: notificationRuntime, 402: ) 403: } 404: 405: async function handleSessionError( 406: client: OpencodeClient, 407: sessionID: string, 408: error: string | undefined, 409: config: NotifyConfig, 410: terminalInfo: TerminalInfo, 411: notificationRuntime: NotificationRuntime, 412: ): Promise<void> { 413: // Check if we should notify for this session 414: if (!config.notifyChildSessions) { 415: const isParent = await isParentSession(client, sessionID) 416: if (!isParent) return 417: } 418: 419: // Check quiet hours 420: if (isQuietHours(config)) return 421: 422: // Check if terminal is focused (suppress notification if user is already looking) 423: if (await isTerminalFocused(terminalInfo)) return 424: 425: const errorMessage = error?.slice(0, 100) || "Something went wrong" 426: 427: await sendNotification( 428: { 429: title: "Something went wrong", 430: message: errorMessage, 431: sound: config.sounds.error, 432: terminalInfo, 433: }, 434: notificationRuntime, 435: ) 436: } 437: 438: async function handlePermissionUpdated( 439: config: NotifyConfig, 440: terminalInfo: TerminalInfo, 441: notificationRuntime: NotificationRuntime, 442: ): Promise<void> { 443: // Always notify for permission events - AI is blocked waiting for human 444: // No parent check needed: permissions always need human attention 445: 446: // Check quiet hours 447: if (isQuietHours(config)) return 448: 449: // Check if terminal is focused (suppress notification if user is already looking) 450: if (await isTerminalFocused(terminalInfo)) return 451: 452: await sendNotification( 453: { 454: title: "Waiting for you", 455: message: "OpenCode needs your input", 456: sound: config.sounds.permission, 457: terminalInfo, 458: }, 459: notificationRuntime, 460: ) 461: } 462: 463: async function handleQuestionAsked( 464: config: NotifyConfig, 465: terminalInfo: TerminalInfo, 466: notificationRuntime: NotificationRuntime, 467: ): Promise<void> { 468: // Guard: quiet hours only (no focus check for questions - tmux workflow) 469: if (isQuietHours(config)) return 470: 471: const sound = config.sounds.question ?? config.sounds.permission 472: 473: await sendNotification( 474: { 475: title: "Question for you", 476: message: "OpenCode needs your input", 477: sound, 478: terminalInfo, 479: }, 480: notificationRuntime, 481: ) 482: } 483: 484: // ========================================== 485: // PLUGIN EXPORT 486: // ========================================== 487: 488: export const NotifyPlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => { 489: const { client } = ctx 490: 491: // Load config once at startup 492: const config = await loadConfig() 493: 494: // Detect terminal once at startup (cached for performance) 495: const terminalInfo = await detectTerminalInfo(config) 496: const notificationRuntime: NotificationRuntime = { 497: preferCmux: canUseCmuxNotification(), 498: } 499: const recentQuestionNotifications: RecentNotifications = new Map() 500: const recentReadyNotifications: RecentNotifications = new Map() 501: const recentPermissionNotifications: RecentNotifications = new Map() 502: 503: const notifyQuestionIfNeeded = async (dedupeKey: string | null): Promise<void> => { 504: if ( 505: dedupeKey && 506: !shouldSendDedupedNotification( 507: recentQuestionNotifications, 508: dedupeKey, 509: QUESTION_DEDUPE_WINDOW_MS, 510: ) 511: ) { 512: return 513: } 514: 515: await handleQuestionAsked(config, terminalInfo, notificationRuntime) 516: } 517: 518: const notifySessionReadyIfNeeded = async (sessionID: unknown): Promise<void> => { 519: const normalizedSessionID = toNonEmptyString(sessionID) 520: if (!normalizedSessionID) return 521: 522: const dedupeKey = buildSessionReadyDedupeKey(normalizedSessionID) 523: if (!dedupeKey) return 524: 525: if ( 526: !shouldSendDedupedNotification(recentReadyNotifications, dedupeKey, READY_DEDUPE_WINDOW_MS) 527: ) { 528: return 529: } 530: 531: await handleSessionIdle( 532: client as OpencodeClient, 533: normalizedSessionID, 534: config, 535: terminalInfo, 536: notificationRuntime, 537: ) 538: } 539: 540: const notifyPermissionIfNeeded = async (properties: unknown): Promise<void> => { 541: const dedupeKey = buildPermissionEventDedupeKey(properties) 542: 543: if ( 544: dedupeKey && 545: !shouldSendDedupedNotification( 546: recentPermissionNotifications, 547: dedupeKey, 548: PERMISSION_DEDUPE_WINDOW_MS, 549: ) 550: ) { 551: return 552: } 553: 554: await handlePermissionUpdated(config, terminalInfo, notificationRuntime) 555: } 556: 557: return { 558: "tool.execute.before": async (input: { tool: string; sessionID: string; callID: string }) => { 559: if (input.tool === "question") { 560: await notifyQuestionIfNeeded(buildQuestionToolDedupeKey(input.sessionID, input.callID)) 561: } 562: }, 563: event: async ({ event }: { event: Event }): Promise<void> => { 564: const runtimeEvent = event as { type: string; properties: Record<string, unknown> } 565: 566: switch (runtimeEvent.type) { 567: case "session.status": { 568: const sessionID = runtimeEvent.properties.sessionID 569: const statusType = 570: runtimeEvent.properties.status && typeof runtimeEvent.properties.status === "object" 571: ? ((runtimeEvent.properties.status as { type?: string }).type ?? undefined) 572: : undefined 573: 574: if (sessionID && statusType === "idle") { 575: await notifySessionReadyIfNeeded(sessionID) 576: } 577: break 578: } 579: case "session.idle": { 580: await notifySessionReadyIfNeeded(runtimeEvent.properties.sessionID) 581: break 582: } 583: case "session.error": { 584: const sessionID = toNonEmptyString(runtimeEvent.properties.sessionID) 585: const error = runtimeEvent.properties.error 586: const errorMessage = typeof error === "string" ? error : error ? String(error) : undefined 587: if (sessionID) { 588: await handleSessionError( 589: client as OpencodeClient, 590: sessionID, 591: errorMessage, 592: config, 593: terminalInfo, 594: notificationRuntime, 595: ) 596: } 597: break 598: } 599: 600: case "permission.updated": 601: case "permission.asked": { 602: await notifyPermissionIfNeeded(runtimeEvent.properties) 603: break 604: } 605: case "question.asked": { 606: const dedupeKey = buildQuestionEventDedupeKey(runtimeEvent.properties) 607: await notifyQuestionIfNeeded(dedupeKey) 608: break 609: } 610: } 611: }, 612: } 613: } "@opencode-ai/plugin" 5: import { z } from "zod" 6: import { getProjectId } from "./kdco-primitives/get-project-id" 7: 8: // ========================================== 9: // PLAN SCHEMA & VALIDATION 10: // ========================================== 11: 12: const PhaseStatus = z.enum(["PENDING", "IN PROGRESS", "COMPLETE", "BLOCKED"]) 13: 14: const TaskSchema = z.object({ 15: id: z.string().regex(/^\d+\.\d+$/, "Task ID must be hierarchical (e.g., '2.1')"), 16: checked: z.boolean(), 17: content: z.string().min(1, "Task content cannot be empty"), 18: isCurrent: z.boolean().optional(), 19: citation: z 20: .string() 21: .regex(/^ref:[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z]+$/, "Citation must be ref:word-word-word format") 22: .optional(), 23: }) 24: 25: const PhaseSchema =
