one task"), 30: }) 31: 32: const FrontmatterSchema = z.object({ 33: status: z.enum(["not-started", "in-progress", "complete", "blocked"]), 34: phase: z.number().int().positive(), 35: updated: z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/, "Date must be YYYY-MM-DD"), 36: }) 37: 38: const PlanSchema = z.object({ 39: frontmatter: FrontmatterSchema, 40: goal: z.string().min(10, "Goal must be at least 10 characters"), 41: context: z 42: .array( 43: z.object({ 44: decision: z.string(), 45: rationale: z.string(), 46: source: z.string(), 47: }), 48: ) 49: .optional(), 50: phases: z.array(PhaseSchema).min(1, "Plan must have at least one phase"), 51: }) 52: 53: /** 54: * Result type for plan parsing - either valid data or descriptive error. 55: * Follows Law 2: Parse Don't Validate - boundary parsing returns trusted types. 56: */ 57: type ParseResult = 58: | { ok: true; data: z.infer<typeof PlanSchema>; warnings: string[] } 59: | { ok: false; error: string; hint: string } 60: 61: /** 62: * Raw extracted parts from markdown (no validation). 63: * Used as intermediate type before Zod validation. 64: */ 65: interface ExtractedParts { 66: frontmatter: Record<string, string | number> | null 67: goal: string | null 68: phases: Array<{ 69: number: number 70: name: string 71: status: string 72: tasks: Array<{ 73: id: string 74: checked: boolean 75: content: string 76: isCurrent: boolean 77: citation?: string 78: }> 79: }> 80: } 81: 82: /** 83: * Extract all parts from markdown without validation (Law 2: Parse Don't Validate). 84: * Returns raw extracted data - validation happens in parsePlanMarkdown. 85: * This is a pure extraction function (Law 3: Purity). 86: */ 87: function extractMarkdownParts(content: string): ExtractedParts { 88: // Extract frontmatter (no validation - just extraction) 89: const fmMatch = content.match(/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---/) 90: let frontmatter: Record<string, string | number> | null = null 91: 92: if (fmMatch) { 93: frontmatter = {} 94: const fmLines = fmMatch[1].split("\n") 95: for (const line of fmLines) { 96: const [key, ...valueParts] = line.split(":") 97: if (key && valueParts.length > 0) { 98: const value = valueParts.join(":").trim() 99: frontmatter[key.trim()] = key.trim() === "phase" ? parseInt(value, 10) : value 100: } 101: } 102: } 103: 104: // Extract goal (no validation - just extraction) 105: const goalMatch = content.match(/## Goal\n([^\n#]+)/) 106: const goal = goalMatch?.[1]?.trim() || null 107: 108: // Extract phases (no validation - just extraction) 109: const phases: ExtractedParts["phases"] = [] 110: const phaseRegex = 111: /## Phase (\d+): ([^[]+)\[([^\]]+)\]\n([\s\S]*?)(?=## Phase \d+:|## Notes|## Blockers|$)/g 112: 113: let phaseMatch = phaseRegex.exec(content) 114: while (phaseMatch !== null) { 115: const phaseNum = parseInt(phaseMatch[1], 10) 116: const phaseName = phaseMatch[2].trim() 117: const phaseStatus = phaseMatch[3].trim() 118: const phaseContent = phaseMatch[4] 119: 120: const tasks: ExtractedParts["phases"][0]["tasks"] = [] 121: const taskRegex = 122: /- \[([ x])\] (\*\*)?(\d+\.\d+) ([^←\n]+)(← CURRENT)?.*?(`ref:[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z]+`)?/g 123: 124: let taskMatch = taskRegex.exec(phaseContent) 125: while (taskMatch !== null) { 126: tasks.push({ 127: id: taskMatch[3], 128: checked: taskMatch[1] === "x", 129: content: taskMatch[4].trim().replace(/\*\*/g, ""), 130: isCurrent: !!taskMatch[5], 131: citation: taskMatch[6]?.replace(/`/g, ""), 132: }) 133: taskMatch = taskRegex.exec(phaseContent) 134: } 135: 136: // Include phase even if no tasks (let Zod validate) 137: phases.push({ 138: number: phaseNum, 139: name: phaseName, 140: status: phaseStatus, 141: tasks, 142: }) 143: phaseMatch = phaseRegex.exec(content) 144: } 145: 146: return { frontmatter, goal, phases } 147: } 148: 149: /** 150: * Format Zod validation errors into human-readable messages (Law 4: Fail Loud). 151: * Shows ALL errors at once with clear paths. 152: */ 153: function formatZodErrors(error: z.ZodError): string { 154: const errorMessages: string[] = [] 155: 156: for (const issue of error.issues) { 157: const path = issue.path.length > 0 ? `[${issue.path.join(".")}]` : "[root]" 158: 159: // Provide helpful context based on error type 160: let message = issue.message 161: if (issue.code === "invalid_value") { 162: const values = (issue as { values?: unknown[] }).values 163: const input = (issue as { input?: unknown }).input 164: message = `Invalid value "${input}". Expected: ${values?.join(" | ") ?? "valid value"}` 165: } else if (issue.code === "invalid_type" && (issue as { input?: unknown }).input === null) { 166: message = "Required field missing" 167: } 168: 169: errorMessages.push(`${path}: ${message}`) 170: } 171: 172: return errorMessages.join("\n") 173: } 174: 175: /** 176: * Parse and validate markdown plan in a single boundary operation. 177: * Returns ParseResult: either trusted data or descriptive error with hint. 178: * 179: * Follows all 5 Laws: 180: * - Law 1 (Early Exit): Guard at top for empty content 181: * - Law 2 (Parse Don't Validate): Extract all → validate once at end 182: * - Law 3 (Purity): No side effects, same input = same output 183: * - Law 4 (Fail Loud): Shows ALL validation errors with clear paths 184: * - Law 5 (Intentional Naming): Self-documenting function names 185: */ 186: function parsePlanMarkdown(content: string): ParseResult { 187: const skillHint = "Load skill('plan-protocol') for the full format spec." 188: 189: // Guard: Content must be string (Law 1: Early Exit, Law 2: Parse at boundary) 190: if (typeof content !== "string") { 191: return { 192: ok: false, 193: error: `Expected markdown string, received ${typeof content}`, 194: hint: skillHint, 195: } 196: } 197: 198: // Guard: Empty content (Law 1: Early Exit) 199: if (!content.trim()) { 200: return { 201: ok: false, 202: error: "Empty content provided", 203: hint: skillHint, 204: } 205: } 206: 207: // Extract all parts without validation (Law 2: Parse Don't Validate) 208: const parts = extractMarkdownParts(content) 209: 210: // Build candidate object for validation 211: const candidate = { 212: frontmatter: parts.frontmatter, 213: goal: parts.goal, 214: phases: parts.phases, 215: } 216: 217: // Single validation point: Zod schema (Law 2: Parse Don't Validate) 218: const result = PlanSchema.safeParse(candidate) 219: if (!result.success) { 220: return { 221: ok: false, 222: error: formatZodErrors(result.error), 223: hint: skillHint, 224: } 225: } 226: 227: // Business rules validation (still part of single boundary) 228: const warnings: string[] = [] 229: let currentCount = 0 230: let inProgressCount = 0 231: 232: for (const phase of result.data.phases) { 233: if (phase.status === "IN PROGRESS") inProgressCount++ 234: for (const task of phase.tasks) { 235: if (task.isCurrent) currentCount++ 236: } 237: } 238: 239: if (currentCount > 1) { 240: return { 241: ok: false, 242: error: `Multiple tasks marked ← CURRENT (found ${currentCount}). Only one task may be current.`, 243: hint: skillHint, 244: } 245: } 246: 247: if (inProgressCount > 1) { 248: warnings.push("Multiple phases marked IN PROGRESS. Consider focusing on one phase at a time.") 249: } 250: 251: return { ok: true, data: result.data, warnings } 252: } 253: 254: /** 255: * Format parse error with actionable guidance (Law 4: Fail Loud). 256: * Includes error message, example, and skill hint. 257: */ 258: function formatParseError(error: string, hint: string): string { 259: return `❌ Plan validation failed: 260: 261: ${error} 262: 263: 💡 ${hint}` 264: } 265: 266: /** 267: * Type guard for Node.js filesystem errors (ENOENT, EACCES, etc.) 268: * Follows "Parse, Don't Validate" - handle uncertainty at boundaries. 269: */ 270: function isNodeError(error: unknown): error is NodeJS.ErrnoException { 271: return error instanceof Error && "code" in error 272: } 273: 274: /** 275: * Expected input for experimental.chat.system.transform hook. 276: * Note: The official SDK types this as {}, but runtime provides these properties. 277: * See: https://github.com/sst/opencode/issues/6142 278: */ 279: interface SystemTransformInput { 280: agent?: string 281: sessionID?: string 282: } 283: 284: /** 285: * KDCO Workspace Plugin 286: * 287: * Provides plan management and targeted rule injection. 288: * Research functionality has been moved to the delegation system (background-agents). 289: * Follows "Elegant Defense" philosophy: Flat, Safe, and Fast. 290: */ 291: 292: // ========================================== 293: // CODER TASK TRACKING FOR REVIEW TRIGGER 294: // ========================================== 295: 296: /** Tracks in-flight coder task callIDs with timestamps for stale cleanup */ 297: const activeCoderCalls = new Map<string, { startTime: number }>() 298: 299: /** Stale call timeout - matches MAX_RUN_TIME_MS in background-agents.ts */ 300: const STALE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000 301: 302: /** Periodic cleanup of orphaned callIDs (runs every 60s) */ 303: const cleanupInterval = setInterval(() => { 304: const now = Date.now() 305: for (const [callID, data] of activeCoderCalls) { 306: if (now - data.startTime > STALE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS) { 307: activeCoderCalls.delete(callID) 308: } 309: } 310: }, 60_000) 311: // Prevent interval from keeping process alive 312: cleanupInterval.unref?.() 313: 314: // ========================================== 315: // RULES FOR INJECTION 316: // ========================================== 317: 318: const PLAN_RULES = `<system-reminder> 319: <workspace-routing policy_level="critical"> 320: 321: ## Agent Routing (STRICT BOUNDARIES) 322: 323: | Agent | Scope | Use For | 324: |-------|-------|---------| 325: | \`explore\` | **INTERNAL ONLY** - codebase files | Find files, understand code structure, trace logic | 326: | \`researcher\` | **EXTERNAL ONLY** - outside codebase | Documentation, websites, npm packages, APIs, tutorials | 327: | \`scribe\` | Human-facing content | Documentation drafts, commit messages, PR descriptions | 328: 329: ## Critical Constraints 330: 331: **You are a READ-ONLY orchestrator. You coordinate research, you do NOT search yourself.** 332: 333: - \`explore\` CANNOT access external resources (docs, web, APIs) 334: - \`researcher\` CANNOT search codebase files 335: - For external docs about a library used in the codebase → \`researcher\` 336: - For how that library is used in THIS codebase → \`explore\` 337: 338: <example> 339: User: "What does the OpenAI API say about function calling?" 340: Correct: delegate to researcher (EXTERNAL - API documentation) 341: Wrong: Try to answer from memory or use MCP tools directly 342: </example> 343: 344: <example> 345: User: "Where is the auth middleware in this project?" 346: Correct: delegate to explore (INTERNAL - codebase search) 347: Wrong: Use grep/glob directly 348: </example> 349: 350: <example> 351: User: "How should I implement OAuth2 in this project?" 352: Correct: 353: 1. delegate to researcher for OAuth2 best practices (EXTERNAL) 354: 2. delegate to explore for existing auth patterns (INTERNAL) 355: Wrong: Search codebase yourself or answer from memory 356: </example> 357: 358: </workspace-routing> 359: 360: <philosophy> 361: Load relevant skills before finalizing plan: 362: - Planning work → \`skill\` load \`plan-protocol\` (REQUIRED before using plan_save) 363: - Backend/logic work → \`skill\` load \`code-philosophy\` 364: - UI/frontend work → \`skill\` load \`frontend-philosophy\` 365: </philosophy> 366: 367: <plan-format> 368: Use \`plan_save\` to save your implementation plan as markdown. 369: 370: ### Format 371: \`\`\`markdown 372: --- 373: status: in-progress 374: phase: 2 375: updated: YYYY-MM-DD 376: --- 377: 378: # Implementation Plan 379: 380: ## Goal 381: [One sentence describing the outcome] 382: 383: ## Context & Decisions 384: | Decision | Rationale | Source | 385: |----------|-----------|--------| 386: | [choice] | [why] | \`ref:delegation-id\` | 387: 388: ## Phase 1: [Name] [COMPLETE] 389: - [x] 1.1 Task description 390: - [x] 1.2 Another task → \`ref:delegation-id\` 391: 392: ## Phase 2: [Name] [IN PROGRESS] 393: - [x] 2.1 Completed task 394: - [ ] **2.2 Current task** ← CURRENT 395: - [ ] 2.3 Pending task 396: \`\`\` 397: 398: ### Rules 399: 1. **One CURRENT task** - Only one task may have ← CURRENT 400: 2. **Cite decisions** - Use \`ref:delegation-id\` for research-informed choices 401: 3. **Update immediately** - Mark tasks complete right after finishing 402: 4. **Auto-save after approval** - When user approves your plan, immediately call \`plan_save\`. Do NOT wait for user to remind you or switch modes. 403: </plan-format> 404: 405: <instruction name="plan_persistence" policy_level="critical"> 406: 407: ## Plan Mode Active 408: You are in PLAN MODE. Your primary deliverable is a saved implementation plan. 409: 410: ## Requirements 411: 1. **First**: Load the \`plan-protocol\` skill to understand the required plan schema 412: 2. **During**: Collaborate with the user to develop a comprehensive, well-cited plan 413: 3. **Before exiting**: You MUST call \`plan_save\` with the finalized plan 414: 415: ## CRITICAL 416: Saving your plan is a REQUIREMENT, not a request. Plans that are not saved will be lost when the session ends or mode changes. The user cannot see your plan unless you save it. 417: 418: </instruction> 419: </system-reminder>` 420: 421: const BUILD_RULES = `<system-reminder> 422: <delegation-mandate policy_level="critical"> 423: 424: ## You Are an ORCHESTRATOR 425: 426: You coordinate work. You do NOT implement. 427: 428: **CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:** 429: - ALL code changes → delegate to \`coder\` 430: - ALL documentation → delegate to \`scribe\` 431: - Codebase questions → delegate to \`explore\` (INTERNAL only) 432: - External docs/APIs → delegate to \`researcher\` (EXTERNAL only) 433: 434: **You may directly:** 435: - Read files for quick context 436: 437: **You may NOT:** 438: - Edit or write any files 439: - Run bash commands (delegate verification to \`coder\`) 440: 441: ## Verification Workflow 442: For any command execution (bun check, bun test, git operations): 443: 1. Delegate to \`coder\` with specific instructions 444: 2. Coder runs commands and reports results 445: 3. You interpret results and decide next actions 446: 447: \`coder\` is your execution proxy for ALL bash operations. 448: 449: </delegation-mandate> 450: 451: <workspace-routing policy_level="critical"> 452: 453: ## Agent Routing (STRICT BOUNDARIES) 454: 455: | Agent | Scope | Use For | 456: |-------|-------|---------| 457: | \`explore\` | **INTERNAL ONLY** - codebase files | Find files, understand code structure, trace logic | 458: | \`researcher\` | **EXTERNAL ONLY** - outside codebase | Documentation, websites, npm packages, APIs, tutorials | 459: | \`coder\` | Implementation | Write/edit code, run builds and tests | 460: | \`scribe\` | Human-facing content | Documentation, commit messages, PR descriptions | 461: 462: ## Boundary Rules 463: 464: - \`explore\` CANNOT access external resources (docs, web, APIs) 465: - \`researcher\` CANNOT search codebase files 466: - \`coder\` handles ALL code modifications 467: - \`scribe\` handles ALL human-facing content 468: 469: </workspace-routing> 470: 471: <build-workflow> 472: 473: ### Before Writing Code 474: 1. Call \`plan_read\` to get the current plan 475: 2. Call \`delegation_list\` ONCE to see available research 476: 3. Call \`delegation_read\` for relevant findings 477: 4. **REUSE code snippets from researcher research** - they are production-ready 478: 479: ### Philosophy Loading 480: Load the relevant skill BEFORE delegating to coder: 481: - Frontend work → \`skill\` load \`frontend-philosophy\` 482: - Backend work → \`skill\` load \`code-philosophy\` 483: 484: ### Execution 485: 1. Orient: Read plan with \`plan_read\` and check delegation findings 486: 2. Load: Load relevant philosophy skill(s) 487: 3. Delegate: Send implementation tasks to \`coder\` 488: 4. Verify: Check coder's results, run \`bun check\` if needed 489: 5. Document: Delegate doc updates to \`scribe\` 490: 6. Update: Mark tasks complete in plan 491: 492: </build-workflow> 493: 494: <code-review-protocol> 495: 496: ## Code Review Protocol 497: 498: When implementation is complete (all plan steps done OR user's request fulfilled): 499: 1. BEFORE reporting completion to the user 500: 2. Delegate to \`reviewer\` agent with the list of changed files 501: 3. Include review findings in your completion report 502: 4. If critical (🔴) or major (🟠) issues found, offer to fix them 503: 504: Do NOT skip this step. Do NOT ask permission to review. 505: The user expects reviewed code, not just implemented code. 506: 507: Review triggers: 508: - All plan tasks marked complete 509: - User's implementation request fulfilled 510: - Before saying "done" or "complete" 511: 512: </code-review-protocol> 513: </system-reminder>` 514: 515: export const WorkspacePlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => { 516: const { directory } = ctx 517: 518: // Use git root commit hash for cross-worktree consistency 519: const projectId = await getProjectId(directory) 520: const baseDir = path.join(os.homedir(), ".local", "share", "opencode", "workspace", projectId) 521: 522: /** 523: * Resolves the root session ID by walking up the parent chain. 524: */ 525: async function getRootSessionID(sessionID?: string): Promise<string> { 526: if (!sessionID) { 527: throw new Error("sessionID is required to resolve root session scope") 528: } 529: 530: let currentID = sessionID 531: for (let depth = 0; depth < 10; depth++) { 532: const session = await ctx.client.session.get({ 533: path: { id: currentID }, 534: }) 535: 536: if (!session.data?.parentID) { 537: return currentID 538: } 539: 540: currentID = session.data.parentID 541: } 542: 543: throw new Error("Failed to resolve root session: maximum traversal depth exceeded") 544: } 545: 546: return { 547: tool: { 548: plan_save: tool({ 549: description: 550: "Save the implementation plan as markdown. Must include citations (ref:delegation-id) for decisions based on research. Plan is validated before saving.", 551: args: { 552: content: tool.schema.string().describe("The full plan in markdown format"), 553: }, 554: async execute(args, toolCtx) { 555: // Guard 1: Session required (Law 1: Early Exit) 556: if (!toolCtx?.sessionID) { 557: return "❌ plan_save requires sessionID. This is a system error." 558: } 559: 560: const rootID = await getRootSessionID(toolCtx.sessionID) 561: const sessionDir = path.join(baseDir, rootID) 562: await fs.mkdir(sessionDir, { recursive: true }) 563: 564: // Guard 2: Parse and validate at boundary (Law 2: Parse Don't Validate) 565: const result = parsePlanMarkdown(args.content) 566: if (!result.ok) { 567: return formatParseError(result.error, result.hint) 568: } 569: 570: // Happy path: save 571: await fs.writeFile(path.join(sessionDir, "plan.md"), args.content, "utf8") 572: const warningCount = result.warnings?.length ?? 0 573: const warningText = 574: warningCount > 0 ? ` (${warningCount} warnings: ${result.warnings?.join(", ")})` : "" 575: 576: return `Plan saved.${warningText}` 577: }, 578: }), 579: 580: plan_read: tool({ 581: description: "Read the current implementation plan for this session.", 582: args: { 583: reason: tool.schema 584: .string() 585: .describe("Brief explanation of why you are calling this tool"), 586: }, 587: async execute(_args, toolCtx) { 588: // Guard: Session required (Law 1: Early Exit) 589: if (!toolCtx?.sessionID) { 590: return "❌ plan_read requires sessionID. This is a system error." 591: } 592: const rootID = await getRootSessionID(toolCtx.sessionID) 593: const planPath = path.join(baseDir, rootID, "plan.md") 594: try { 595: return await fs.readFile(planPath, "utf8") 596: } catch (error) { 597: if (isNodeError(error) && error.code === "ENOENT") return "No plan found." 598: throw error 599: } 600: }, 601: }), 602: }, 603: 604: // Targeted Rule Injection 605: "experimental.chat.system.transform": async (input: SystemTransformInput, output) => { 606: const agent = input.agent 607: 608: // Universal date awareness (all agents) - Law 2: Parse intent, not just data 609: const today = new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0] 610: output.system.push(`<date-awareness> 611: Today is ${today}. When searching for documentation, APIs, or external resources, use the current year (${new Date().getFullYear()}). Do not default to outdated years from training data. 612: </date-awareness>`) 613: 614: // Agent-specific rules 615: if (agent === "plan") { 616: output.system.push(PLAN_RULES) 617: } else if (agent === "build") { 618: output.system.push(BUILD_RULES) 619: } 620: }, 621: 622: // Track coder task starts for review trigger 623: "tool.execute.before": async ( 624: input: { tool: string; callID?: string }, 625: output: { args?: { subagent_type?: string } }, 626: ) => { 627: if (input.tool !== "task") return 628: if (!input.callID) return 629: if (output.args?.subagent_type !== "coder") return 630: 631: activeCoderCalls.set(input.callID, { startTime: Date.now() }) 632: }, 633: 634: // Trigger review reminder when plan_save or all coder tasks complete 635: "tool.execute.after": async ( 636: input: { tool: string; sessionID: string; callID: string }, 637: output: { title: string; output: string; metadata: unknown }, 638: ) => { 639: // Plan save triggers reviewer delegation reminder 640: if (input.tool === "plan_save") { 641: output.output += `\n\n<system-reminder> 642: Plan saved successfully. You MUST now delegate to the reviewer: 643: 1. Use the \`delegate\` tool to send the plan to the \`reviewer\` agent 644: 2. The reviewer will load \`plan-review\` and \`code-philosophy\` skills 645: 3. Use \`plan_read\` to get the plan content for the delegation prompt 646: 4. This is NON-BLOCKING - continue work while review runs in background 647: </system-reminder>` 648: return 649: } 650: 651: // Coder task completion tracking 652: if (!input.callID) return 653: if (!activeCoderCalls.has(input.callID)) return 654: 655: activeCoderCalls.delete(input.callID) 656: 657: if (activeCoderCalls.size === 0) { 658: output.output += `\n\n<system-reminder> 659: Coder task complete. Proceed to code review: 660: 1. Delegate to \`reviewer\` agent with the changed files 661: 2. Include findings in your completion report 662: 3. Offer to fix any critical/major issues found 663: </system-reminder>` 664: } 665: }, 666: 667: // Compaction Hook - Inject plan context when session is compacted 668: "experimental.session.compacting": async ( 669: input: { sessionID: string }, 670: output: { context: string[]; prompt?: string }, 671: ) => { 672: const rootID = await getRootSessionID(input.sessionID) 673: const planPath = path.join(baseDir, rootID, "plan.md") 674: 675: let planContent: string | null = null 676: try { 677: planContent = await fs.readFile(planPath, "utf8") 678: } catch (error) { 679: if (!isNodeError(error) || error.code !== "ENOENT") throw error 680: } 681: 682: if (!planContent) return 683: 684: // Extract current task from plan 685: const currentMatch = planContent.match(/← CURRENT/) 686: let currentTask: string | null = null 687: if (currentMatch?.index !== undefined) { 688: const start = Math.max(0, currentMatch.index - 100) 689: const end = currentMatch.index + 50 690: currentTask = planContent.slice(start, end).match(/\d+\.\d+ [^\n←]+/)?.[0] ?? null 691: } 692: 693: output.context.push(`<workspace-context> 694: ## Current Plan 695: ${planContent} 696: 697: ## Resume Point 698: ${currentTask ? `Current task: ${currentTask}` : "No task marked as CURRENT"} 699: 700: ## Verification 701: To verify any cited decision, use \`delegation_read("ref:id")\`. 702: </workspace-context>`) 703: }, 704: } 705: } 706: 707: export default WorkspacePlugin 1: /** 2: * OCX Worktree Plugin 3: * 4: * Creates isolated git worktrees for AI development sessions with 5: * seamless terminal spawning across macOS, Windows, and Linux. 6: * 7: * Inspired by opencode-worktree-session by Felix Anhalt 8: * https://github.com/felixAnhalt/opencode-worktree-session 9: * License: MIT 10: * 11: * Rewritten for OCX with production-proven patterns. 12: */ 13: 14: import type { Database } from "bun:sqlite" 15:
