"node:path" 19: import { type Plugin, tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin" 20: import type { Event } from "@opencode-ai/sdk" 21: import type { OpencodeClient } from "./kdco-primitives/types" 22: 23: /** Logger interface for structured logging */ 24: interface Logger { 25: debug: (msg: string) => void 26: info: (msg: string) => void 27: warn: (msg: string) => void 28: error: (msg: string) => void 29: } 30: 31: import { parse as parseJsonc } from "jsonc-parser" 32: import { z } from "zod" 33: 34: import { getProjectId } from "./kdco-primitives/get-project-id" 35: import { 36: type ActiveLaunchContext, 37: buildSessionLaunchArgv, 38: parseActiveLaunchContext, 39: serializePersistedLaunchMetadata, 40: toPersistedLaunchMetadata, 41: } from "./worktree/launch-context" 42: import { 43: addSession, 44: clearPendingDelete, 45: getPendingDelete, 46: getSession, 47: getWorktreePath, 48: initStateDb, 49: removeSession, 50: setPendingDelete, 51: } from "./worktree/state" 52: import { openTerminal, type TerminalResult } from "./worktree/terminal" 53: 54: /** Maximum retries for database initialization */ 55: const DB_MAX_RETRIES = 3 56: 57: /** Delay between retry attempts in milliseconds */ 58: const DB_RETRY_DELAY_MS = 100 59: 60: /** Maximum depth to traverse session parent chain */ 61: const MAX_SESSION_CHAIN_DEPTH = 10 62: 63: // ============================================================================= 64: // TYPES & SCHEMAS 65: // ============================================================================= 66: 67: /** Result type for fallible operations */ 68: interface OkResult<T> { 69: readonly ok: true 70: readonly value: T 71: } 72: interface ErrResult<E> { 73: readonly ok: false 74: readonly error: E 75: } 76: type Result<T, E> = OkResult<T> | ErrResult<E> 77: 78: const Result = { 79: ok: <T>(value: T): OkResult<T> => ({ ok: true, value }), 80: err: <E>(error: E): ErrResult<E> => ({ ok: false, error }), 81: } 82: 83: /** 84: * Git branch name validation - blocks invalid refs and shell metacharacters 85: * Characters blocked: control chars (0x00-0x1f, 0x7f), ~^:?*[]\\, and shell metacharacters 86: */ 87: function isValidBranchName(name: string): boolean { 88: // Check for control characters 89: for (let i = 0; i < name.length; i++) { 90: const code = name.charCodeAt(i) 91: if (code <= 0x1f || code === 0x7f) return false 92: } 93: // Check for invalid git ref characters and shell metacharacters 94: if (/[~^:?*[\]\\;&|`$()]/.test(name)) return false 95: return true 96: } 97: 98: const branchNameSchema = z 99: .string() 100: .min(1, "Branch name cannot be empty") 101: .refine((name) => !name.startsWith("-"), { 102: message: "Branch name cannot start with '-' (prevents option injection)", 103: }) 104: .refine((name) => !name.startsWith("/") && !name.endsWith("/"), { 105: message: "Branch name cannot start or end with '/'", 106: }) 107: .refine((name) => !name.includes("//"), { 108: message: "Branch name cannot contain '//'", 109: }) 110: .refine((name) => !name.includes("@{"), { 111: message: "Branch name cannot contain '@{' (git reflog syntax)", 112: }) 113: .refine((name) => !name.includes(".."), { 114: message: "Branch name cannot contain '..'", 115: }) 116: // biome-ignore lint/suspicious/noControlCharactersInRegex: Control character detection is intentional for security 117: .refine((name) => !/[\x00-\x1f\x7f ~^:?*[\]\\]/.test(name), { 118: message: "Branch name contains invalid characters", 119: }) 120: .max(255, "Branch name too long") 121: .refine((name) => isValidBranchName(name), "Contains invalid git ref characters") 122: .refine((name) => !name.startsWith(".") && !name.endsWith("."), "Cannot start or end with dot") 123: .refine((name) => !name.endsWith(".lock"), "Cannot end with .lock") 124: 125: /** 126: * Worktree plugin configuration schema. 127: * Config file: .opencode/worktree.jsonc 128: */ 129: const worktreeConfigSchema = z.object({ 130: /** Custom base path for worktree storage. Supports ~ for home directory. */ 131: worktreePath: z.string().optional(), 132: sync: z 133: .object({ 134: /** Files to copy from main worktree (relative paths only) */ 135: copyFiles: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 136: /** Directories to symlink from main worktree (saves disk space) */ 137: symlinkDirs: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 138: /** Patterns to exclude from copying (reserved for future use) */ 139: exclude: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 140: }) 141: .default(() => ({ copyFiles: [], symlinkDirs: [], exclude: [] })), 142: hooks: z 143: .object({ 144: /** Commands to run after worktree creation */ 145: postCreate: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 146: /** Commands to run before worktree deletion */ 147: preDelete: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 148: }) 149: .default(() => ({ postCreate: [], preDelete: [] })), 150: }) 151: 152: type WorktreeConfig = z.infer<typeof worktreeConfigSchema> 153: 154: // ============================================================================= 155: // ERROR TYPES 156: // ============================================================================= 157: 158: class WorktreeError extends Error { 159: constructor( 160: message: string, 161: public readonly operation: string, 162: public readonly cause?: unknown, 163: ) { 164: super(`${operation}: ${message}`) 165: this.name = "WorktreeError" 166: } 167: } 168: 169: type ResolveExecutable = (command: string) => string | null | undefined 170: type ValidateProfileAvailability = ( 171: ocxBin: string, 172: profile: string, 173: ) => Promise<Result<void, string>> 174: 175: interface LaunchExecutableValidationOptions { 176: resolveExecutable?: ResolveExecutable 177: pathExists?: (absolutePath: string) => Promise<boolean> 178: } 179: 180: export function isPathLikeCommand(command: string): boolean { 181: return command.includes("/") || command.includes("\\") 182: } 183: 184: function resolveStableLaunchBinaryPath( 185: ocxBin: string, 186: baseDirectory: string, 187: resolveExecutable: ResolveExecutable, 188: ): Result<string, string> { 189: if (isPathLikeCommand(ocxBin)) { 190: const resolvedPath = path.isAbsolute(ocxBin) ? ocxBin : path.resolve(baseDirectory, ocxBin) 191: return Result.ok(resolvedPath) 192: } 193: 194: const resolvedFromPath = resolveExecutable(ocxBin) 195: if (!resolvedFromPath) { 196: return Result.err(`Configured OCX binary "${ocxBin}" is not available in PATH.`) 197: } 198: 199: const resolvedPath = path.isAbsolute(resolvedFromPath) 200: ? resolvedFromPath 201: : path.resolve(baseDirectory, resolvedFromPath) 202: 203: return Result.ok(resolvedPath) 204: } 205: 206: async function pathPointsToLaunchableBinary(absolutePath: string): Promise<boolean> { 207: try { 208: const stats = await stat(absolutePath) 209: if (stats.isDirectory()) { 210: return false 211: } 212: 213: await access(absolutePath, fsConstants.X_OK) 214: return true 215: } catch { 216: return false 217: } 218: } 219: 220: export async function ensureLaunchContextExecutable( 221: launchContext: ActiveLaunchContext, 222: baseDirectory: string, 223: options: LaunchExecutableValidationOptions = {}, 224: ): Promise<ActiveLaunchContext> { 225: if (launchContext.mode === "plain") { 226: return launchContext 227: } 228: 229: const { ocxBin, profile } = launchContext 230: const resolveExecutable = options.resolveExecutable ?? ((command: string) => Bun.which(command)) 231: const pathExists = options.pathExists ?? pathPointsToLaunchableBinary 232: const resolvedPathResult = resolveStableLaunchBinaryPath(ocxBin, baseDirectory, resolveExecutable) 233: if (!resolvedPathResult.ok) { 234: throw new WorktreeError( 235: `${resolvedPathResult.error} Repair the parent OCX profile (${profile}) and recreate this worktree session.`, 236: "launch", 237: ) 238: } 239: 240: const resolvedPath = resolvedPathResult.value 241: const isLaunchable = await pathExists(resolvedPath) 242: if (!isLaunchable) { 243: throw new WorktreeError( 244: `Configured OCX binary "${ocxBin}" resolved to "${resolvedPath}" but is missing or stale. Repair the parent OCX profile (${profile}) and recreate this worktree session.`, 245: "launch", 246: ) 247: } 248: 249: return { 250: mode: "ocx", 251: ocxBin: resolvedPath, 252: profile, 253: } 254: } 255: 256: export async function validateOcxProfileAvailability( 257: ocxBin: string, 258: profile: string, 259: ): Promise<Result<void, string>> { 260: try { 261: const proc = Bun.spawn([ocxBin, "profile", "show", profile, "--global", "--json"], { 262: stdout: "pipe", 263: stderr: "pipe", 264: }) 265: const [exitCode, stdout, stderr] = await Promise.all([ 266: proc.exited, 267: new Response(proc.stdout).text(), 268: new Response(proc.stderr).text(), 269: ]) 270: 271: if (exitCode === 0) { 272: return Result.ok(undefined) 273: } 274: 275: const detail = stderr.trim() || stdout.trim() || `exit ${exitCode}` 276: return Result.err(detail) 277: } catch (error) { 278: return Result.err(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)) 279: } 280: } 281: 282: export async function ensureLaunchContextProfile( 283: launchContext: ActiveLaunchContext, 284: validateProfileAvailability: ValidateProfileAvailability = validateOcxProfileAvailability, 285: ): Promise<void> { 286: if (launchContext.mode === "plain") { 287: return 288: } 289: 290: const validationResult = await validateProfileAvailability( 291: launchContext.ocxBin, 292: launchContext.profile, 293: ) 294: if (validationResult.ok) { 295: return 296: } 297: 298: throw new WorktreeError( 299: `Configured OCX profile "${launchContext.profile}" is missing or stale. ${validationResult.error} Repair the parent OCX profile and recreate this worktree session.`, 300: "launch", 301: ) 302: } 303: 304: // ============================================================================= 305: // SESSION FORKING HELPERS 306: // ============================================================================= 307: 308: /** 309: * Check if a path exists, distinguishing ENOENT from other errors (Law 4) 310: */ 311: async function pathExists(filePath: string): Promise<boolean> { 312: try { 313: await access(filePath) 314: return true 315: } catch (e: unknown) { 316: if (e && typeof e === "object" && "code" in e && e.code === "ENOENT") { 317: return false 318: } 319: throw e // Re-throw permission errors, etc. 320: } 321: } 322: 323: /** 324: * Copy file if source exists. Returns true if copied, false if source doesn't exist. 325: * Throws on copy failure (Law 4: Fail Loud) 326: */ 327: async function copyIfExists(src: string, dest: string): Promise<boolean> { 328: if (!(await pathExists(src))) return false 329: await copyFile(src, dest) 330: return true 331: } 332: 333: /** 334: * Copy directory contents if source exists. 335: * @param src - Source directory path 336: * @param dest - Destination directory path 337: * @returns true if copy was performed, false if source doesn't exist 338: */ 339: async function copyDirIfExists(src: string, dest: string): Promise<boolean> { 340: if (!(await pathExists(src))) return false 341: await cp(src, dest, { recursive: true }) 342: return true 343: } 344: 345: interface ForkResult { 346: forkedSession: { id: string } 347: rootSessionId: string 348: planCopied: boolean 349: delegationsCopied: boolean 350: } 351: 352: interface FinalizeWorktreeLaunchOptions { 353: database: Database 354: worktreePath: string 355: launchArgv: string[] 356: branch: string 357: forkedSessionId: string 358: sessionRecord: { 359: id: string 360: branch: string 361: path: string 362: createdAt: string 363: launchMode: "plain" | "ocx" 364: profile: string | null 365: ocxBin: string | null 366: } 367: log: Logger 368: openTerminalFn?: (cwd: string, argv?: string[], windowName?: string) => Promise<TerminalResult> 369: addSessionFn?: typeof addSession 370: deleteForkedSessionFn?: (sessionId: string) => Promise<void> 371: } 372: 373: export async function finalizeWorktreeLaunch( 374: options: FinalizeWorktreeLaunchOptions, 375: ): Promise<TerminalResult> { 376: const openTerminalFn = options.openTerminalFn ?? openTerminal 377: const addSessionFn = options.addSessionFn ?? addSession 378: const deleteForkedSessionFn = 379: options.deleteForkedSessionFn ?? 380: (async (_sessionId: string) => { 381: // Default no-op for tests without cleanup side effects. 382: }) 383: 384: const terminalResult = await openTerminalFn( 385: options.worktreePath, 386: options.launchArgv, 387: options.branch, 388: ) 389: 390: if (!terminalResult.success) { 391: await deleteForkedSessionFn(options.forkedSessionId).catch((cleanupError) => { 392: options.log.warn( 393: `[worktree] Failed to clean up forked session ${options.forkedSessionId} after launch failure: ${cleanupError}`, 394: ) 395: }) 396: return terminalResult 397: } 398: 399: addSessionFn(options.database, options.sessionRecord) 400: return terminalResult 401: } 402: 403: /** 404: * Fork a session and copy associated plans/delegations. 405: * Cleans up forked session on failure (atomic operation). 406: */ 407: async function forkWithContext( 408: client: OpencodeClient, 409: sessionId: string, 410: projectId: string, 411: getRootSessionIdFn: (sessionId: string) => Promise<string>, 412: ): Promise<ForkResult> { 413: // Guard clauses (Law 1) 414: if (!client) throw new WorktreeError("client is required", "forkWithContext") 415: if (!sessionId) throw new WorktreeError("sessionId is required", "forkWithContext") 416: if (!projectId) throw new WorktreeError("projectId is required", "forkWithContext") 417: 418: // Get root session ID with error wrapping 419: let rootSessionId: string 420: try { 421: rootSessionId = await getRootSessionIdFn(sessionId) 422: } catch (e) { 423: throw new WorktreeError("Failed to get root session ID", "forkWithContext", e) 424: } 425: 426: // Fork session 427: const forkedSessionResponse = await client.session.fork({ 428: path: { id: sessionId }, 429: body: {}, 430: }) 431: const forkedSession = forkedSessionResponse.data 432: if (!forkedSession?.id) { 433: throw new WorktreeError("Failed to fork session: no session data returned", "forkWithContext") 434: } 435: 436: // Copy data with cleanup on failure 437: let planCopied = false 438: let delegationsCopied = false 439: 440: try { 441: const workspaceBase = path.join(os.homedir(), ".local", "share", "opencode", "workspace") 442: const delegationsBase = path.join(os.homedir(), ".local", "share", "opencode", "delegations") 443: 444: const destWorkspaceDir = path.join(workspaceBase, projectId, forkedSession.id) 445: const destDelegationsDir = path.join(delegationsBase, projectId, forkedSession.id) 446: 447: await mkdir(destWorkspaceDir, { recursive: true }) 448: await mkdir(destDelegationsDir, { recursive: true }) 449: 450: // Copy plan 451: const srcPlan = path.join(workspaceBase, projectId, rootSessionId, "plan.md") 452: const destPlan = path.join(destWorkspaceDir, "plan.md") 453: planCopied = await copyIfExists(srcPlan, destPlan) 454: 455: // Copy delegations 456: const srcDelegations = path.join(delegationsBase, projectId, rootSessionId) 457: delegationsCopied = await copyDirIfExists(srcDelegations, destDelegationsDir) 458: } catch (error) { 459: client.app 460: .log({ 461: body: { 462: service: "worktree", 463: level: "error", 464: message: `forkWithContext: Copy failed, cleaning up forked session: ${error}`, 465: }, 466: }) 467: .catch(() => {}) 468: // Clean up orphaned directories 469: const workspaceBase = path.join(os.homedir(), ".local", "share", "opencode", "workspace") 470: const delegationsBase = path.join(os.homedir(), ".local", "share", "opencode", "delegations") 471: const destWorkspaceDir = path.join(workspaceBase, projectId, forkedSession.id) 472: const destDelegationsDir = path.join(delegationsBase, projectId, forkedSession.id) 473: await rm(destWorkspaceDir, { recursive: true, force: true }).catch((e) => { 474: client.app 475: .log({ 476: body: { 477: service: "worktree", 478: level: "error", 479: message: `forkWithContext: Failed to clean up workspace dir ${destWorkspaceDir}: ${e}`, 480: }, 481: }) 482: .catch(() => {}) 483: }) 484: await rm(destDelegationsDir, { recursive: true, force: true }).catch((e) => { 485: client.app 486: .log({ 487: body: { 488: service: "worktree", 489: level: "error", 490: message: `forkWithContext: Failed to clean up delegations dir ${destDelegationsDir}: ${e}`, 491: }, 492: }) 493: .catch(() => {}) 494: }) 495: await client.session.delete({ path: { id: forkedSession.id } }).catch((e) => { 496: client.app 497: .log({ 498: body: { 499: service: "worktree", 500: level: "error", 501: message: `forkWithContext: Failed to clean up forked session ${forkedSession.id}: ${e}`, 502: }, 503: }) 504: .catch(() => {}) 505: }) 506: throw new WorktreeError( 507: `Failed to copy session data: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`, 508: "forkWithContext", 509: error, 510: ) 511: } 512: 513: return { forkedSession, rootSessionId, planCopied, delegationsCopied } 514: } 515: 516: // ============================================================================= 517: // MODULE-LEVEL STATE 518: // ============================================================================= 519: 520: /** Database instance - initialized once per plugin lifecycle */ 521: let db: Database | null = null 522: 523: /** Project root path - stored on first initialization */ 524: let projectRoot: string | null = null 525: 526: /** Flag to prevent duplicate cleanup handler registration */ 527: let cleanupRegistered = false 528: 529: /** 530: * Register process cleanup handlers for graceful database shutdown. 531: * Ensures WAL checkpoint and proper close on process termination. 532: * 533: * NOTE: process.once() is an EventEmitter method that never throws. 534: * The boolean guard is defense-in-depth for idempotency, not error recovery. 535: * 536: * @param database - The database instance to clean up 537: */ 538: function registerCleanupHandlers(database: Database): void { 539: if (cleanupRegistered) return // Early exit guard 540: cleanupRegistered = true 541: 542: const cleanup = () => { 543: try { 544: database.exec("PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)") 545: database.close() 546: } catch { 547: // Best effort cleanup - process is exiting anyway 548: } 549: } 550: 551: process.once("SIGTERM", cleanup) 552: process.once("SIGINT", cleanup) 553: process.once("beforeExit", cleanup) 554: } 555: 556: /** 557: * Get the database instance, initializing if needed. 558: * Includes retry logic for transient initialization failures. 559: * 560: * @returns Database instance 561: * @throws {Error} if initialization fails after all retries 562: */ 563: async function getDb(log: Logger): Promise<Database> { 564: if (db) return db 565: 566: if (!projectRoot) { 567: throw new Error("Database not initialized: projectRoot not set. Call initDb() first.") 568: } 569: 570: let lastError: Error | null = null 571: 572: for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= DB_MAX_RETRIES; attempt++) { 573: try { 574: db = await initStateDb(projectRoot) 575: registerCleanupHandlers(db) 576: return db 577: } catch (error) { 578: lastError = error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error)) 579: log.warn(`Database init attempt ${attempt}/${DB_MAX_RETRIES} failed: ${lastError.message}`) 580: 581: if (attempt < DB_MAX_RETRIES) { 582: Bun.sleepSync(DB_RETRY_DELAY_MS) 583: } 584: } 585: } 586: 587: throw new Error( 588: `Failed to initialize database after ${DB_MAX_RETRIES} attempts: ${lastError?.message}`, 589: ) 590: } 591: 592: /** 593: * Initialize the database with the project root path. 594: * Must be called once before any getDb() calls. 595: */ 596: async function initDb(root: string, log: Logger): Promise<Database> { 597: projectRoot = root 598: return getDb(log) 599: } 600: 601: // ============================================================================= 602: // GIT MODULE 603: // ============================================================================= 604: 605: /** 606: * Execute a git command safely using Bun.spawn with explicit array. 607: * Avoids shell interpolation entirely by passing args as array. 608: */ 609: async function git(args: string[], cwd: string): Promise<Result<string, string>> { 610: try { 611: const proc = Bun.spawn(["git", ...args], { 612: cwd, 613: stdout: "pipe", 614: stderr: "pipe", 615: }) 616: const [stdout, stderr, exitCode] = await Promise.all([ 617: new Response(proc.stdout).text(), 618: new Response(proc.stderr).text(), 619: proc.exited, 620: ]) 621: if (exitCode !== 0) { 622: return Result.err(stderr.trim() || `git ${args[0]} failed`) 623: } 624: return Result.ok(stdout.trim()) 625: } catch (error) { 626: return Result.err(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)) 627: } 628: } 629: 630: async function branchExists(cwd: string, branch: string): Promise<boolean> { 631: const result = await git(["rev-parse", "--verify", branch], cwd) 632: return result.ok 633: } 634: 635: async function createWorktree( 636: repoRoot: string, 637: branch: string, 638: baseBranch?: string, 639: basePath?: string, 640: ): Promise<Result<string, string>> { 641: const worktreePath = await getWorktreePath(repoRoot, branch, basePath) 642: 643: // Ensure parent directory exists 644: await mkdir(path.dirname(worktreePath), { recursive: true }) 645: 646: const exists = await branchExists(repoRoot, branch) 647: 648: if (exists) { 649: // Checkout existing branch into worktree 650: const result = await git(["worktree", "add", worktreePath, branch], repoRoot) 651: return result.ok ? Result.ok(worktreePath) : result 652: } else { 653: // Create new branch from base 654: const base = baseBranch ?? "HEAD" 655: const result = await git(["worktree", "add", "-b", branch, worktreePath, base], repoRoot) 656: return result.ok ? Result.ok(worktreePath) : result 657: } 658: } 659: 660: async function removeWorktree( 661: repoRoot: string, 662: worktreePath: string, 663: ): Promise<Result<void, string>> { 664: const result = await git(["worktree", "remove", "--force", worktreePath], repoRoot) 665: return result.ok ? Result.ok(undefined) : Result.err(result.error) 666: } 667: 668: // ============================================================================= 669: // FILE SYNC MODULE 670: // ============================================================================= 671: 672: /** 673: * Validate that a path is safe (no escape from base directory) 674: */ 675: function isPathSafe(filePath: string, baseDir: string, log: Logger): boolean { 676: // Reject absolute paths 677: if (path.isAbsolute(filePath)) { 678: log.warn(`[worktree] Rejected absolute path: ${filePath}`) 679: return false 680: } 681: // Reject obvious path traversal 682: if (filePath.includes("..")) { 683: log.warn(`[worktree] Rejected path traversal: ${filePath}`) 684: return false 685: } 686: // Verify resolved path stays within base directory 687: const resolved = path.resolve(baseDir, filePath) 688: if (!resolved.startsWith(baseDir + path.sep) && resolved !== baseDir) { 689: log.warn(`[worktree] Path escapes base directory: ${filePath}`) 690: return false 691: } 692: return true 693: } 694: 695: /** 696: * Copy files from source directory to target directory. 697: * Skips missing files silently (production pattern). 698: */ 699: async function copyFiles( 700: sourceDir: string, 701: targetDir: string, 702: files: string[], 703: log: Logger, 704: ): Promise<void> { 705: for (const file of files) { 706: if (!isPathSafe(file, sourceDir, log)) continue 707: 708: const sourcePath = path.join(sourceDir, file) 709: const targetPath = path.join(targetDir, file) 710: 711: try { 712: const sourceFile = Bun.file(sourcePath) 713: if (!(await sourceFile.exists())) { 714: log.debug(`[worktree] Skipping missing file: ${file}`) 715: continue 716: } 717: 718: // Ensure target directory exists 719: const targetFileDir = path.dirname(targetPath) 720: await mkdir(targetFileDir, { recursive: true }) 721: 722: // Copy file 723: await Bun.write(targetPath, sourceFile) 724: log.info(`[worktree] Copied: ${file}`) 725: } catch (error) { 726: const isNotFound = 727: error instanceof Error && 728: (error.message.includes("ENOENT") || error.message.includes("no such file")) 729: if (isNotFound) { 730: log.debug(`[worktree] Skipping missing: ${file}`) 731: } else { 732: log.warn(`[worktree] Failed to copy ${file}: ${error}`) 733: } 734: } 735: } 736: } 737: 738: /** 739: * Create symlinks for directories from source to target. 740: * Uses absolute paths for symlink targets. 741: */ 742: async function symlinkDirs( 743: sourceDir: string, 744: targetDir: string, 745: dirs: string[], 746: log: Logger, 747: ): Promise<void> { 748: for (const dir of dirs) { 749: if (!isPathSafe(dir, sourceDir, log)) continue 750: 751: const sourcePath = path.join(sourceDir, dir) 752: const targetPath = path.join(targetDir, dir) 753: 754: try { 755: // Check if source directory exists 756: const fileStat = await stat(sourcePath).catch(() => null) 757: if (!fileStat || !fileStat.isDirectory()) { 758: log.debug(`[worktree] Skipping missing directory: ${dir}`) 759: continue 760: } 761: 762: // Ensure parent directory exists 763: const targetParentDir = path.dirname(targetPath) 764: await mkdir(targetParentDir, { recursive: true }) 765: 766: // Remove existing target if it exists (might be empty dir from git) 767: await rm(targetPath, { recursive: true, force: true }) 768: 769: // Create symlink (use absolute path for source) 770: await symlink(sourcePath, targetPath, "dir") 771: log.info(`[worktree] Symlinked: ${dir}`) 772: } catch (error) { 773: log.warn(`[worktree] Failed to symlink ${dir}: ${error}`) 774: } 775: } 776: } 777: 778: /** 779: * Run hook commands in the worktree directory. 780: */ 781: async function runHooks(cwd: string, commands: string[], log: Logger): Promise<void> { 782: for (const command of commands) { 783: log.info(`[worktree] Running hook: ${command}`) 784: try { 785: // Use shell to properly handle quoted arguments and complex commands 786: const result = Bun.spawnSync(["bash", "-c", command], { 787: cwd, 788: stdout: "inherit", 789: stderr: "pipe", 790: }) 791: if (result.exitCode !== 0) { 792: const stderr = result.stderr?.toString() || "" 793: log.warn( 794: `[worktree] Hook failed (exit ${result.exitCode}): ${command}${stderr ? `\n${stderr}` : ""}`, 795: ) 796: } 797: } catch (error) { 798: log.warn(`[worktree] Hook error: ${error}`) 799: } 800: } 801: } 802: 803: /** 804: * Resolve a path that may contain a leading `~` to the user's home directory. 805: */ 806: function resolveHomePath(p: string): string { 807: if (p === "~" || p.startsWith("~/") || p.startsWith("~\\")) { 808: return path.join(os.homedir(), p.slice(1)) 809: } 810: return p 811: } 812: 813: /** 814: * Load worktree-specific configuration from .opencode/worktree.jsonc 815: * Auto-creates config file with helpful defaults if it doesn't exist. 816: */ 817: async function loadWorktreeConfig(directory: string, log: Logger): Promise<WorktreeConfig> { 818: const configPath = path.join(directory, ".opencode", "worktree.jsonc") 819: 820: try { 821: const file = Bun.file(configPath) 822: if (!(await file.exists())) { 823: // Auto-create config with helpful defaults and comments 824: const defaultConfig = `{ 825: "$schema": "https://registry.kdco.dev/schemas/worktree.json", 826: 827: // Worktree plugin configuration 828: // Documentation: https://github.com/kdcokenny/ocx 829: 830: // Custom base path for worktree storage (supports ~) 831: // Default: ~/.local/share/opencode/worktree 832: // "worktreePath": "~/my-worktrees", 833: 834: "sync": { 835: // Files to copy from main worktree to new worktrees 836: // Example: [".env", ".env.local", "dev.sqlite"] 837: "copyFiles": [], 838: 839: // Directories to symlink (saves disk space) 840: // Example: ["node_modules"] 841: "symlinkDirs": [], 842: 843: // Patterns to exclude from copying 844: "exclude": [] 845: }, 846: 847: "hooks": { 848: // Commands to run after worktree creation 849: // Example: ["pnpm install", "docker compose up -d"] 850: "postCreate": [], 851: 852: // Commands to run before worktree deletion 853: // Example: ["docker compose down"] 854: "preDelete": [] 855: } 856: } 857: ` 858: // Ensure .opencode directory exists 859: await mkdir(path.join(directory, ".opencode"), { recursive: true }) 860: await Bun.write(configPath, defaultConfig) 861: log.info(`[worktree] Created default config: ${configPath}`) 862: return worktreeConfigSchema.parse({}) 863: } 864: 865: const content = await file.text() 866: // Use proper JSONC parser (handles comments in strings correctly) 867: const parsed = parseJsonc(content) 868: if (parsed === undefined) { 869: log.error(`[worktree] Invalid worktree.jsonc syntax`) 870: return worktreeConfigSchema.parse({}) 871: } 872: const config = worktreeConfigSchema.parse(parsed) 873: if (config.worktreePath) { 874: config.worktreePath = resolveHomePath(config.worktreePath) 875: } 876: return config 877: } catch (error) { 878: log.warn(`[worktree] Failed to load config: ${error}`) 879: return worktreeConfigSchema.parse({}) 880: } 881: } 882: 883: // ============================================================================= 884: // PLUGIN ENTRY 885: // ============================================================================= 886: 887: export const WorktreePlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => { 888: const { directory, client } = ctx 889: 890: const log = { 891: debug: (msg: string) => 892: client.app 893: .log({ body: { service: "worktree", level: "debug", message: msg } }) 894: .catch(() => {}), 895: info: (msg: string) => 896: client.app 897: .log({ body: { service: "worktree", level: "info", message: msg } }) 898: .catch(() => {}), 899: warn: (msg: string) => 900: client.app 901: .log({ body: { service: "worktree", level: "warn", message: msg } }) 902: .catch(() => {}), 903: error: (msg: string) => 904: client.app 905: .log({ body: { service: "worktree", level: "error", message: msg } }) 906: .catch(() => {}), 907: } 908: 909: // Initialize SQLite database 910: const database = await initDb(directory, log) 911: 912: return { 913: tool: { 914: worktree_create: tool({ 915: description: 916: "Create a new git worktree for isolated development. A new terminal will open with OpenCode in the worktree.", 917: args: { 918: branch: tool.schema 919: .string() 920: .describe("Branch name for the worktree (e.g., 'feature/dark-mode')"), 921: baseBranch: tool.schema 922: .string() 923: .optional() 924: .describe("Base branch to create from (defaults to HEAD)"), 925: }, 926: async execute(args, toolCtx) { 927: // Validate branch name at boundary 928: const branchResult = branchNameSchema.safeParse(args.branch) 929: if (!branchResult.success) { 930: return `❌ Invalid branch name: ${branchResult.error.issues[0]?.message}` 931: } 932: 933: // Validate base branch name at boundary 934: if (args.baseBranch) { 935: const baseResult = branchNameSchema.safeParse(args.baseBranch) 936: if (!baseResult.success) { 937: return `❌ Invalid base branch name: ${baseResult.error.issues[0]?.message}` 938: } 939: } 940: 941: let activeLaunchContext: ActiveLaunchContext 942: try { 943: activeLaunchContext = parseActiveLaunchContext( 944: process.env as Record<string, string | undefined>, 945: ) 946: activeLaunchContext = await ensureLaunchContextExecutable( 947: activeLaunchContext, 948: directory, 949: ) 950: await ensureLaunchContextProfile(activeLaunchContext) 951: } catch (error) { 952: return `❌ ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}` 953: } 954: 955: // Load config first so worktreePath is available for createWorktree 956: const worktreeConfig = await loadWorktreeConfig(directory, log) 957: 958: // Create worktree 959: const result = await createWorktree( 960: directory, 961: args.branch, 962: args.baseBranch, 963: worktreeConfig.worktreePath, 964: ) 965: if (!result.ok) { 966: return `Failed to create worktree: ${result.error}` 967: } 968: 969: const worktreePath = result.value 970: 971: // Sync files from main worktree 972: const mainWorktreePath = directory // The repo root is the main worktree 973: 974: // Copy files 975: if (worktreeConfig.sync.copyFiles.length > 0) { 976: await copyFiles(mainWorktreePath, worktreePath, worktreeConfig.sync.copyFiles, log) 977: } 978: 979: // Symlink directories 980: if (worktreeConfig.sync.symlinkDirs.length > 0) { 981: await symlinkDirs(mainWorktreePath, worktreePath, worktreeConfig.sync.symlinkDirs, log) 982: } 983: 984: // Run postCreate hooks 985: if (worktreeConfig.hooks.postCreate.length > 0) { 986: await runHooks(worktreePath, worktreeConfig.hooks.postCreate, log) 987: } 988: 989: // Fork session with context (replaces --session resume) 990: const projectId = await getProjectId(worktreePath, client) 991: const { forkedSession, planCopied, delegationsCopied } = await forkWithContext( 992: client, 993: toolCtx.sessionID, 994: projectId, 995: async (sid) => { 996: // Walk up parentID chain to find root session 997: let currentId = sid 998: for (let depth = 0; depth < MAX_SESSION_CHAIN_DEPTH; depth++) { 999: const session = await client.session.get({ path: { id: currentId } }) 1000: if (!session.data?.parentID) return currentId 1001: currentId = session.data.parentID 1002: } 1003: return currentId 1004: }, 1005: ) 1006: 1007: log.debug( 1008: `Forked session ${forkedSession.id}, plan: ${planCopied}, delegations: ${delegationsCopied}`, 1009: ) 1010: const persistedLaunchMetadata = toPersistedLaunchMetadata(activeLaunchContext) 1011: const launchArgv = buildSessionLaunchArgv(forkedSession.id, persistedLaunchMetadata) 1012: const serializedLaunchMetadata = serializePersistedLaunchMetadata(persistedLaunchMetadata) 1013: 1014: const terminalResult = await finalizeWorktreeLaunch({ 1015: database, 1016: worktreePath, 1017: launchArgv, 1018: branch: args.branch, 1019: forkedSessionId: forkedSession.id, 1020: sessionRecord: { 1021: id: forkedSession.id, 1022: branch: args.branch, 1023: path: worktreePath, 1024: createdAt: new Date().toISOString(), 1025: launchMode: serializedLaunchMetadata.launchMode, 1026: profile: serializedLaunchMetadata.profile, 1027: ocxBin: serializedLaunchMetadata.ocxBin, 1028: }, 1029: log, 1030: deleteForkedSessionFn: async (sessionId: string) => { 1031: await client.session.delete({ path: { id: sessionId } }) 1032: }, 1033: }) 1034: 1035: if (!terminalResult.success) { 1036: return `❌ Failed to launch worktree terminal: ${terminalResult.error ?? "unknown error"}\nWorktree created at ${worktreePath}. Verify launch settings and retry.` 1037: } 1038: 1039: return `Worktree created at ${worktreePath}\n\nA new terminal has been opened with OpenCode.` 1040: }, 1041: }), 1042: 1043: worktree_delete: tool({ 1044: description: 1045: "Delete the current worktree and clean up. Changes will be committed before removal.", 1046: args: { 1047: reason: tool.schema 1048: .string() 1049: .describe("Brief explanation of why you are calling this tool"), 1050: }, 1051: async execute(_args, toolCtx) { 1052: // Find current session's worktree 1053: const session = getSession(database, toolCtx?.sessionID ?? "") 1054: if (!session) { 1055: return `No worktree associated with this session` 1056: } 1057: 1058: // Set pending delete for session.idle (atomic operation) 1059: setPendingDelete(database, { branch: session.branch, path: session.path }, client) 1060: 1061: return `Worktree marked for cleanup. It will be removed when this session ends.` 1062: }, 1063: }), 1064: }, 1065: 1066: event: async ({ event }: { event: Event }): Promise<void> => { 1067: if (event.type !== "session.idle") return 1068: 1069: // Handle pending delete 1070: const pendingDelete = getPendingDelete(database) 1071: if (pendingDelete) { 1072: const { path: worktreePath, branch } = pendingDelete 1073: 1074: // Run preDelete hooks before cleanup 1075: const config = await loadWorktreeConfig(directory, log) 1076: if (config.hooks.preDelete.length > 0) { 1077: await runHooks(worktreePath, config.hooks.preDelete, log) 1078: } 1079: 1080: // Commit any uncommitted changes 1081: const addResult = await git(["add", "-A"], worktreePath) 1082: if (!addResult.ok) log.warn(`[worktree] git add failed: ${addResult.error}`) 1083: 1084: const commitResult = await git( 1085: ["commit", "-m", "chore(worktree): session snapshot", "--allow-empty"], 1086: worktreePath, 1087: ) 1088: if (!commitResult.ok) log.warn(`[worktree] git commit failed: ${commitResult.error}`) 1089: 1090: // Remove worktree 1091: const removeResult = await removeWorktree(directory, worktreePath) 1092: if (!removeResult.ok) { 1093: log.warn(`[worktree] Failed to remove worktree: ${removeResult.error}`) 1094: } 1095: 1096: // Clear pending delete atomically 1097: clearPendingDelete(database) 1098: 1099: // Remove session from database 1100: removeSession(database, branch) 1101: } 1102: }, 1103: } 1104: } 1105: 1106: export default WorktreePlugin 1: --- 2: name: code-philosophy 3: description: Internal logic and data flow philosophy (The 5 Laws of Elegant Defense). Understand deeply to ensure code guides data naturally and prevents errors. 4: --- 5: 6: # Internal Logic Philosophy: The 5 Laws of Elegant Defense 7: 8: **Role:** Principal Engineer for all **Internal Logic & Data Flow** — applies to backend, React components, hooks, state management, and any code where functionality matters. 9: 10: **Philosophy:** Elegant Simplicity — code should guide data so naturally that errors become impossible, keeping core logic flat, readable, and pristine. 11: 12: ## The 5 Laws 13: 14: ### 1. The Law of the Early Exit (Guard Clauses) 15: - **Concept:** Indentation is the enemy of simplicity. Deep nesting hides bugs. 16: - **Rule:** Handle edge cases, nulls, and errors at the very top of functions. 17: - **Practice:** Use `if (!valid) return; doWork();` instead of `if (valid) { doWork(); }`. 18: 19: ### 2. Make Illegal States Unrepresentable (Parse, Don't Validate) 20: - **Concept:** Don't check data repeatedly; structure it so it can't be wrong. 21: - **Rule:** Parse inputs at the boundary. Once data enters internal logic, it must be in trusted, typed state. 22: - **Why:** Removes defensive checks deep in algorithmic code, keeping core logic pristine. 23: 24: ### 3. The Law of Atomic Predictability 25: - **Concept:** A function must never surprise the caller. 26: - **Rule:** Functions should be "Pure" where possible. Same Input = Same Output. No hidden mutations. 27: - **Defense:** Avoid `void` functions that mutate global state. Return new data structures instead. 28: 29: ### 4. The Law of "Fail Fast, Fail Loud" 30: - **Concept:** Silent failures cause complexity later. 31: - **Rule:** If a state is invalid, halt immediately with a descriptive error. Do not try to "patch" bad data. 32: - **Result:** Keeps logic simple by never accounting for "half-broken" states. 33: 34: ### 5. The Law of Intentional Naming 35: - **Concept:** Comments are often a crutch for bad code. 36: - **Rule:** Variables and functions must be named so clearly that logic reads like an English sentence. 37: - **Defense:** `isUserEligible` is better than `check()`. The name itself guarantees the boolean logic. 38: 39: --- 40: 41: ## Adherence Checklist 42: Before completing your task, verify: 43: - [ ] **Guard Clauses:** Are all edge cases handled at the top with early returns? 44: - [ ] **Parsed State:** Is data parsed into trusted types at the boundary? 45: - [ ] **Purity:** Are functions predictable and free of hidden mutations? 46: - [ ] **Fail Loud:** Do invalid states throw clear, descriptive errors immediately? 47: - [ ] **Readability:** Does the logic read like an English sentence? 1: --- 2: name: code-review 3: description: Comprehensive code review methodology with severity classification and confidence thresholds 4: --- 5: 6: # Code Review Philosophy 7: 8: ## TL;DR 9: Systematic code review across 4 layers with severity classification. Only report findings with ≥80% confidence. Include file:line references for all issues. 10: 11: ## When to Use This Skill 12: - Before reporting implementation completion 13: - When explicitly asked to review code 14: - When using the `/review` command 15: - As an independent audit after code changes 16: 17: ## The 4 Review Layers 18: 19: ### Layer 1: Correctness 20: - Logic errors and edge cases 21: - Error handling completeness 22: - Type safety and null checks 23: - Algorithm correctness 24: - Off-by-one errors 25: 26: ### Layer 2: Security 27: - No hardcoded secrets or API keys 28: - Input validation and sanitization 29: - Injection vulnerability prevention (SQL, XSS, command) 30: - Authentication and authorization checks 31: - Sensitive data not logged 32: - OWASP Top 10 awareness 33: 34: ### Layer 3: Performance 35: - No N+1 query patterns 36: - Appropriate caching strategies 37: - No unnecessary re-renders (React/frontend) 38: - Lazy loading where appropriate 39: - Memory leak prevention 40: - Algorithmic complexity concerns 41: 42: ### Layer 4: Style & Maintainability 43: - Adherence to project conventions (check AGENTS.md) 44: - Code duplication (DRY violations) 45: - Complexity management (cyclomatic complexity) 46: - Documentation completeness 47: - Test coverage gaps 48: 49: ## Severity Classification 50: 51: | Severity | Icon | Criteria | Action Required | 52: |----------|------|----------|-----------------| 53: | Critical | 🔴 | Security vulnerabilities, crashes, data loss, corruption | Must fix before merge | 54: | Major | 🟠 | Bugs, performance issues, missing error handling | Should fix | 55: | Minor | 🟡 | Code smells, maintainability issues, test gaps | Nice to fix | 56: | Nitpick | 🟢 | Style preferences, naming suggestions, documentation | Optional | 57: 58: ## Confidence Threshold 59: 60: **Only report findings with ≥80% confidence.** 61: 62: If uncertain about an issue: 63: - State the uncertainty explicitly: "Potential issue (70% confidence): ..." 64: - Suggest investigation rather than assert a problem 65: - Prefer false negatives over false positives (reduce noise) 66: 67: ## Review Process 68: 69: 1. **Initial Scan** - Identify all files in scope, understand the change 70: 2. **Deep Analysis** - Apply all 4 layers systematically to each file 71: 3. **Context Evaluation** - Consider surrounding code, project patterns, existing conventions 72: 4. **Philosophy Check** - Verify against code-philosophy (5 Laws) if applicable 73: 5. **Synthesize Findings** - Group by severity, deduplicate, prioritize 74: 75: ## Output Format 76: 77: Structure your review as: 78: 79: 1. **Files Reviewed** - List all files analyzed 80: 2. **Overall Assessment** - APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | NEEDS_DISCUSSION 81: 3. **Summary** - 2-3 sentence overview 82: 4. **Critical Issues** (🔴) - With file:line references 83: 5. **Major Issues** (🟠) - With file:line references 84: 6. **Minor Issues** (🟡) - With file:line references 85: 7. **Positive Observations** (🟢) - What's done well (always include at least one) 86: 8. **Philosophy Compliance** - Checklist results if applicable 87: 88: ## What NOT to Do 89: 90: - Do NOT report low-confidence findings as definite issues 91: - Do NOT provide vague feedback without file:line references 92: - Do NOT skip any of the 4 layers 93: - Do NOT forget to note positive observations 94: - Do NOT modify any files during review 95: - Do NOT approve without completing the full review process 96: 97: ## Adherence Checklist 98: 99: Before completing a review, verify: 100: - [ ] All 4 layers analyzed (Correctness, Security, Performance, Style) 101: - [ ] Severity assigned to each finding 102: - [ ] Confidence ≥80% for all reported issues (or uncertainty stated) 103: - [ ] File names and line numbers included for all findings 104: - [ ] Positive observations noted 105: - [ ] Output follows the standard format 1: --- 2: name: frontend-philosophy 3: description: Visual & UI philosophy (The 5 Pillars of Intentional UI). Understand deeply to avoid "AI slop" and create distinctive, memorable interfaces. 4: --- 5: 6: # Frontend Design Philosophy: The 5 Pillars of Intentional UI 7: 8: **Role:** Design Director for all **Visual & Aesthetic decisions** — applies to styling, layout, colors, typography, animations, and UI composition. 9: 10: **Philosophy:** Distinctive, memorable, intentional design — avoiding generic "AI slop" aesthetics through bold, characterful choices that create immediate emotional impact. 11: 12: ## The 5 Pillars 13: 14: ### 1. Typography with Character 15: - **Concept:** Fonts set the entire tone. Generic fonts create generic, forgettable interfaces. 16: - **Rule:** Avoid Inter, Roboto, Arial, and system-ui defaults. Choose distinctive, characterful typefaces. 17: - **Practice:** Pair dramatic display fonts with refined, readable body fonts. 18: 19: ### 2. Committed Color & Theme 20: - **Concept:** Timid palettes lack impact and feel algorithmically generated. 21: - **Rule:** Use bold, dominant colors with sharp accent contrasts. Avoid evenly-distributed rainbow gradients. 22: - **Practice:** Establish CSS variable systems early. Break away from the "purple gradient on white" AI cliché. 23: 24: ### 3. Purposeful Motion 25: - **Concept:** Animation should delight, not distract. Scattered micro-interactions create noise. 26: - **Rule:** One well-orchestrated animation beats a dozen minor transitions. Focus on high-impact moments. 27: - **Practice:** Use CSS animations for HTML, Motion library for React. Prioritize staggered reveals and surprsing hover states. 28: 29: ### 4. Brave Spatial Composition 30: - **Concept:** Predictable layouts are forgettable. Safe spacing feels automated. 31: - **Rule:** Either generous negative space OR controlled density — not the middle ground. 32: - **Practice:** Embrace asymmetry, overlap, diagonal flow, and grid-breaking elements. 33: 34: ### 5. Atmosphere & Depth 35: - **Concept:** Flat solid backgrounds lack presence and feel unfinished. 36: - **Rule:** Layer visual richness through gradient meshes, noise textures, geometric patterns, and transparencies. 37: - **Practice:** Add dramatic shadows, decorative borders, grain overlays. 38: 39: --- 40: 41: ## Adherence Checklist 42: Before completing your task, verify: 43: - [ ] **Typography:** Did you avoid generic system fonts? 44: - [ ] **Color:** Are the color choices bold and intentional? 45: - [ ] **Motion:** Is there a primary, high-impact animation? 46: - [ ] **Space:** Does the layout feel designed rather than templated? 47: - [ ] **Depth:** Is there visual richness (textures, gradients, layering)? 1: --- 2: name: plan-protocol 3: description: Guidelines for creating and managing implementation plans with citations 4: --- 5: 6: # Plan Protocol 7: 8: > **Load this skill** when creating or updating implementation plans. 9: 10: ## TL;DR Checklist 11: 12: When creating or updating a plan, ensure: 13: 14: - [ ] YAML frontmatter with `status`, `phase`, `updated` 15: - [ ] `## Goal` section (one sentence) 16: - [ ] `## Context & Decisions` table with citations (`ref:delegation-id`) 17: - [ ] Phases with status markers: `[COMPLETE]`, `[IN PROGRESS]`, `[PENDING]` 18: - [ ] Tasks with hierarchical numbering (1.1, 1.2, 2.1) 19: - [ ] Only ONE task marked `← CURRENT` 20: - [ ] Citations for all research-based decisions 21: 22: --- 23: 24: ## When to Use 25: 26: 1. Starting a multi-step implementation 27: 2. After receiving a complex user request 28: 3. When tracking progress across phases 29: 4. After research that informs architectural decisions 30: 31: ## When NOT to Use 32: 33: 1. Simple one-off tasks → use built-in todos instead 34: 2. Pure research/exploration → use delegations only 35: 3. Quick fixes that don't need tracking 36: 4. Single-file changes with no dependencies 37: 38: --- 39: 40: ## Plan Format 41: 42: Use `plan_save` with this exact markdown format: 43: 44: ```markdown 45: --- 46: status: STATUS 47: phase: PHASE_NUMBER 48: updated: YYYY-MM-DD 49: --- 50: 51: # Implementation Plan 52: 53: ## Goal 54: ONE_SENTENCE_DESCRIBING_OUTCOME 55: 56: ## Context & Decisions 57: | Decision | Rationale | Source | 58: |----------|-----------|--------| 59: | CHOICE | WHY | `ref:DELEGATION_ID` | 60: 61: ## Phase 1: NAME [STATUS_MARKER] 62: - [x] 1.1 Completed task 63: - [x] 1.2 Another completed task → `ref:DELEGATION_ID` 64: 65: ## Phase 2: NAME [IN PROGRESS] 66: - [x] 2.1 Completed task 67: - [ ] **2.2 Current task** ← CURRENT 68: - [ ] 2.3 Pending task 69: 70: ## Phase 3: NAME [PENDING] 71: - [ ] 3.1 Future task 72: - [ ] 3.2 Another future task 73: 74: ## Notes 75: - YYYY-MM-DD: Observation or decision `ref:DELEGATION_ID` 76: ``` 77: 78: ### Frontmatter Fields 79: 80: | Field | Values | Description | 81: |-------|--------|-------------| 82: | `status` | `not-started`, `in-progress`, `complete`, `blocked` | Overall plan status | 83: | `phase` | Number (1, 2, 3...) | Current phase number | 84: | `updated` | `YYYY-MM-DD` | Last update date | 85: 86: ### Phase Status Markers 87: 88: | Marker | Meaning | 89: |--------|---------| 90: | `[PENDING]` | Not yet started | 91: | `[IN PROGRESS]` | Currently being worked on | 92: | `[COMPLETE]` | Finished successfully | 93: | `[BLOCKED]` | Waiting on dependencies | 94: 95: --- 96: 97: ## State Machine 98: 99: ### Plan Lifecycle 100: ``` 101: not-started → in-progress → complete 102: ↘ blocked 103: ``` 104: 105: ### Phase Lifecycle 106: ``` 107: [PENDING] → [IN PROGRESS] → [COMPLETE] 108: ↘ [BLOCKED] 109: ``` 110: 111: ### Task Lifecycle 112: ``` 113: [ ] unchecked → [x] checked 114: ``` 115: 116: ### Critical Rules 117: 118: 1. **Only ONE phase** may be `[IN PROGRESS]` at any time 119: 2. **Only ONE task** may have `← CURRENT` marker at any time 120: 3. **Move `← CURRENT`** immediately when starting a new task 121: 4. **Mark tasks `[x]`** immediately after completing them 122: 123: --- 124: 125: ## Citations & Delegations 126: 127: ### Where Citations Come From 128: 129: Citations reference delegation research. The flow is: 130: 131: 1. You delegate research: `delegate` to `researcher` or `explore` 132: 2. Delegation completes with a readable ID (e.g., `swift-amber-falcon`) 133: 3. You cite that research in the plan: `ref:swift-amber-falcon` 134: 135: ### When to Cite 136: 137: | Situation | Action | 138: |-----------|--------| 139: | Architectural decision based on research | Add to Context & Decisions table | 140: | Task informed by research | Append `→ ref:id` to task line | 141: | Implementation detail from research | Inline citation in Notes | 142: 143: ### How to Find Delegation IDs 144: 145: - Use `delegation_list()` to see all delegations 146: - Use `delegation_read("id")` to verify content before citing 147: 148: ### ❌ NEVER 149: 150: - Make up delegation IDs 151: - Cite without actually reading the delegation 152: - Skip citations for research-based decisions 153: 154: --- 155: 156: ## Examples 157: 158: ### ✅ CORRECT: Well-formed plan 159: 160: ```markdown 161: --- 162: status: in-progress 163: phase: 2 164: updated: 2026-01-02 165: --- 166: 167: # Implementation Plan 168: 169: ## Goal 170: Add JWT authentication with refresh token support 171: 172: ## Context & Decisions 173: | Decision | Rationale | Source | 174: |----------|-----------|--------| 175: | Use bcrypt (12 rounds) | Industry standard, balance of security/speed | `ref:swift-amber-falcon` | 176: | JWT with refresh tokens | Stateless auth, mobile-friendly | `ref:calm-jade-owl` | 177: 178: ## Phase 1: Research [COMPLETE] 179: - [x] 1.1 Research auth patterns → `ref:swift-amber-falcon` 180: - [x] 1.2 Evaluate token strategies → `ref:calm-jade-owl` 181: 182: ## Phase 2: Implementation [IN PROGRESS] 183: - [x] 2.1 Set up project structure 184: - [ ] **2.2 Add password hashing** ← CURRENT 185: - [ ] 2.3 Implement JWT generation 186: 187: ## Phase 3: Testing [PENDING] 188: - [ ] 3.1 Write unit tests 189: - [ ] 3.2 Integration tests 190: 191: ## Notes 192: - 2026-01-02: Chose bcrypt over argon2 for broader library support `ref:swift-amber-falcon` 193: ``` 194: 195: ### ❌ WRONG: Missing frontmatter 196: 197: ```markdown 198: # Implementation Plan 199: 200: ## Goal 201: Add authentication 202: ``` 203: 204: **Error:** Plan must have YAML frontmatter with status, phase, updated. 205: 206: ### ❌ WRONG: Multiple CURRENT markers 207: 208: ```markdown 209: ## Phase 2: Implementation [IN PROGRESS] 210: - [ ] **2.1 Task one** ← CURRENT 211: - [ ] **2.2 Task two** ← CURRENT 212: ``` 213: 214: **Error:** Only one task may be marked CURRENT. 215: 216: ### ❌ WRONG: Decision without citation 217: 218: ```markdown 219: ## Context & Decisions 220: | Decision | Rationale | Source | 221: |----------|-----------|--------| 222: | Use Redis | It's fast | - | 223: ``` 224: 225: **Error:** Decisions must cite research with `ref:delegation-id`. 226: 227: ### ❌ WRONG: Invalid phase status 228: 229: ```markdown 230: ## Phase 1: Research [DONE] 231: ``` 232: 233: **Error:** Use `[COMPLETE]`, not `[DONE]`. Valid markers: `[PENDING]`, `[IN PROGRESS]`, `[COMPLETE]`, `[BLOCKED]`. 234: 235: --- 236: 237: ## Troubleshooting 238: 239: | Error Message | Fix | 240: |---------------|-----| 241: | "Missing frontmatter" | Add `---\nstatus: in-progress\nphase: 1\nupdated: 2026-01-02\n---` at top | 242: | "Multiple CURRENT markers" | Remove `← CURRENT` from all but the active task | 243: | "Invalid citation format" | Use `ref:delegation-id` format (e.g., `ref:swift-amber-falcon`) | 244: | "Missing goal" | Add `## Goal` section with one-sentence description | 245: | "Empty phase" | Add at least one task to each phase | 246: | "Invalid phase status" | Use `[PENDING]`, `[IN PROGRESS]`, `[COMPLETE]`, or `[BLOCKED]` | 247: 248: --- 249: 250: ## Before Saving Checklist 251: 252: Before calling `plan_save`, verify: 253: 254: - [ ] **Frontmatter:** Has status, phase, and updated date? 255: - [ ] **Goal:** Is there a clear, one-sentence goal? 256: - [ ] **Citations:** Are all research-based decisions cited with `ref:id`? 257: - [ ] **Single CURRENT:** Is exactly one task marked `← CURRENT`? 258: - [ ] **Valid markers:** Do all phases use valid status markers? 259: - [ ] **Hierarchical IDs:** Are tasks numbered correctly (1.1, 1.2, 2.1)? 1: --- 2: name: plan-review 3: description: Criteria for reviewing implementation plans against quality standards 4: --- 5: 6: # Plan Review 7: 8: > **Load this skill** when reviewing implementation plans (not code). 9: 10: ## TL;DR 11: Systematic plan review focused on 3 quality categories: Citation Quality, Completeness, and Actionability. Structure is pre-validated by `plan_save`—focus on whether the plan provides actionable implementation guidance. 12: 13: ## When to Use This Skill 14: - When reviewing implementation plans before execution 15: - When auditing plan quality after creation 16: - When verifying plans meet documentation standards 17: - As part of the plan validation workflow 18: 19: --- 20: 21: ## Plan Review Checklist 22: 23: ### 1. Structure (Pre-validated) 24: 25: > **Note:** Saved plans are structurally validated by `plan_save` before storage. 26: > Format compliance (YAML frontmatter, status markers, CURRENT marker, numbering) is guaranteed. 27: > Focus your review on the quality aspects below. 28: 29: ### 2. Citation Quality 30: 31: | Requirement | Check | 32: |-------------|-------| 33: | Decisions reference sources | `ref:delegation-id` format used | 34: | No unsubstantiated claims | Architectural decisions cite research | 35: | Research phases show refs | Completed research tasks include citations | 36: | Citations are verifiable | IDs match actual delegation outputs | 37: 38: **Red Flags:** 39: - Decisions table with empty or `-` in Source column 40: - Claims like "industry standard" or "best practice" without citation 41: - Research tasks marked complete without `→ ref:id` 42: 43: ### 3. Completeness 44: 45: | Requirement | Check | 46: |-------------|-------| 47: | Goal is specific | Measurable outcome, not vague intent | 48: | Phases are logical | Sequential, with clear progression | 49: | Edge cases considered | Error handling, failure modes addressed | 50: | Notes section present | Key decisions and observations documented | 51: | Context & Decisions table | Captures architectural choices with rationale | 52: 53: **Goal Quality Examples:** 54: - ❌ "Improve authentication" (vague) 55: - ❌ "Make it better" (unmeasurable) 56: - ✅ "Add JWT authentication with refresh token support" (specific) 57: - ✅ "Migrate user table to PostgreSQL with zero downtime" (measurable) 58: 59: ### 4. Actionability 60: 61: | Requirement | Check | 62: |-------------|-------| 63: | Tasks are specific | Clear what file/component is affected | 64: | No ambiguous tasks | Avoids "investigate" or "figure out" without scope | 65: | Dependencies clear | Sequential tasks show logical order | 66: | Implementation path obvious | Developer can start without clarification | 67: 68: **Actionability Examples:** 69: - ❌ "Set up the backend" (too vague) 70: - ❌ "Make it work" (no implementation path) 71: - ✅ "Create `src/auth/jwt.ts` with sign/verify functions" (specific file) 72: - ✅ "Add bcrypt password hashing to `UserService.create()`" (clear scope) 73: 74: --- 75: 76: ## Severity Classification 77: 78: | Severity | Icon | Criteria | Action Required | 79: |----------|------|----------|-----------------| 80: | Critical | 🔴 | Missing citations for key decisions, no clear goal, unactionable tasks | Must fix before execution | 81: | Major | 🟠 | Vague tasks, incomplete phases, missing edge case handling | Should fix | 82: | Minor | 🟡 | Missing notes, unclear dependencies, incomplete rationale | Nice to fix | 83: | Nitpick | 🟢 | Style preferences, wording suggestions | Optional | 84: 85: --- 86: 87: ## Output Format 88: 89: Structure your plan review as: 90: 91: ```markdown 92: ## Plan Review 93: 94: ### Files Reviewed 95: - `PLAN.md` (or plan content from `plan_read`) 96: 97: ### Overall Assessment 98: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | NEEDS_DISCUSSION 99: 100: ### Summary 101: 2-3 sentence overview of plan quality. 102: 103: ### Issues 104: 105: #### 🔴 Critical 106: - [Issue description with specific location] 107: 108: #### 🟠 Major 109: - [Issue description with specific location] 110: 111: #### 🟡 Minor 112: - [Issue description with specific location] 113: 114: #### 🟢 Nitpick 115: - [Suggestion] 116: 117: ### Quality Assessment 118: 119: | Check | Status | 120: |-------|--------| 121: | Goal is specific and measurable | PASS / FAIL | 122: | Citations support key decisions | PASS / FAIL | 123: | Tasks are actionable | PASS / FAIL | 124: | Edge cases addressed | PASS / FAIL | 125: 126: ### Positive Observations 127: - [What's done well - always include at least one] 128: ``` 129: 130: --- 131: 132: ## What NOT to Do 133: 134: - Do NOT re-validate format—`plan_save` handles structural validation 135: - Do NOT evaluate code quality (that's code-review's job) 136: - Do NOT execute or modify the plan during review 137: - Do NOT skip citation verification for decisions 138: - Do NOT accept vague goals or ambiguous tasks 139: - Do NOT forget to note positive observations 140: 141: --- 142: 143: ## Adherence Checklist 144: 145: Before completing a plan review, verify: 146: 147: - [ ] All 3 quality categories analyzed (Citations, Completeness, Actionability) 148: - [ ] Severity assigned to each finding 149: - [ ] Specific locations noted for all issues 150: - [ ] Quality Assessment table completed 151: - [ ] Positive observations noted 152: - [ ] Output follows the standard format 1: --- 2: name: code-philosophy 3: description: Internal logic and data flow philosophy (The 5 Laws of Elegant Defense). Understand deeply to ensure code guides data naturally and prevents errors. 4: --- 5: 6: # Internal Logic Philosophy: The 5 Laws of Elegant Defense 7: 8: **Role:** Principal Engineer for all **Internal Logic & Data Flow** — applies to backend, React components, hooks, state management, and any code where functionality matters. 9: 10: **Philosophy:** Elegant Simplicity — code should guide data so naturally that errors become impossible, keeping core logic flat, readable, and pristine. 11: 12: ## The 5 Laws 13: 14: ### 1.
